The PHS COLOR BOOK  
 
 
This book is a transcription of the PHS Practitioner Program, Variation II, Year Two, 
Semester Three that was originally taught in the Spring of 2008.  The transcript 
contains all PHS External Color and Tone material taught except for specific 
references regarding graduation requirements of this program.

Introduction to External 
Effectiveness and Longevity 
 
 
Hello to everyone, welcome to you and I am glad to be here.  It‟s interesting for me.  
After all, you've been going through the recordings that I did in relationship to the 
chains, so we haven‟t had a chance to actually really be in a classroom together in a 
while.  The area of work that you've been focused on over the last two semesters is 
a foundation.  That is, it is a foundation for being able to have the facility to 
understand the movement of information.  Chains are enormously important.  And 
now we have to step back into what is perhaps the most complex areas of the 
knowledge.  I think what you'll find is that this semester and particularly the first 
semester of the third year is as complex as we get.  The beginning of it is here. 
 
The Nodes 
It‟s interesting for me because this is the second time I do this and will be the only 
time that I will do this again.  So, it's an interesting revisit to what is for me is an 
extraordinary theme and one that has really become the foundation of my 
understanding of the nature of being.  What I'm referring to is the Nodes.  You‟ll 
notice this is something that has really been left out of your instruction over the 
previous five semesters.  It is very, very complex and basically all of that 
background you have is a foundation of being able to approach this. 
 
Let's begin with our process.  As you can see, we have an introduction to the 
external and you have a sub-theme that we will explore deeper and deeper as we 
move along in this process.  You can see the way in which the theme is expressed.  
That is, effectiveness and longevity.  It begins with understanding something about 
what has happened to us physically since 1781 and that is the emergence of this 
nine-centered vehicle.  This nine-centered vehicle is very different.  It is a 
transitionary form.  And as a transitional form it has something of the past and it has 
something of the future.

The Saturnian Cycle and the Uranian Cycle 
But there is something very basic to understand about the nature of this vehicle.  
And it‟s best understood by an understanding of the seven-centered being as being 
Saturnian and the nine-centered being as being Uranian.  Many of you know that the 
advent of the nine-centered being is coincidental to the discovery of Uranus by 
Herschel.  We have two very different cycles.  When you‟re looking at the Saturnian 
cycle you‟re looking at basically 30 years.  You look at the Uranian cycle and you're 
looking at somewhere around 84 years.  That‟s an enormous difference in terms of 
the relative way in which we look at time relative to our life spans. 
 
 
 
Most of the history of humanity, the approximate 85,000 years since the mutation of 
the dropping of the larynx and the advent of what was the full mental potential of 
the seven-centered being, its left orientation, its development and expansion was of 
the powers of mind.  After all, it was a seven-centered being that developed what we 
have to clean up.  That is, the distortion of a world that is decided by mind. 
 
Pre 1781 Seven-Centered Uranian Cycle—84 years 
Post 1781 Nine-Centered Saturnian Cycle—30 years

Introduction to External

The Difference between the Seven-Centered and the Nine-Centered Body 
But the bodies were very different.  One of the most interesting things that I know 
about the difference between the seven-centered and the nine-centered being is that 
the nine-centered being is hotter.  Our body temperature, our natural standard body 
temperature as a transitional form in this context, we run warmer than the seven-
centered being.  But what really is something to consider is—these vehicles are like a 
factory that produces a product and tells you more or less how long the product is 
good for.  This product should be good for four years, five years, or six years, 
whatever the case may be.  And basically that is a statement of potential successful 
functionality.  In other words, how long the thing can run effectively.   
 
Basically we have the same thing if you look at us in terms of our Saturnian and 
Uranian bodies.  The Saturnian body came out with a stamp on it that said “good till 
30.”  After 30, who knows, but good until 30.  And it‟s really something that we have 
lost all sense of it.  It is hard for us to imagine, even though we have evidence of 
that in the most disenfranchised societies on this planet in terms of the life 
expectancy of somebody, in Burundi as an example, where there is maybe 36 or 37 
years, most of which is simply a by-product of AIDS.   
 
But, we don't really understand it conceptually as a product of Western culture; we 
just don't.  This understanding that life used to be always incredibly short.  When 
you think about the Saturnian vehicle, its half-life, the Saturnian opposition, is going 
to be around 15 years of age.  It‟s only when you understand the Saturnian body in 
the Saturnian half-life that you can even begin to understand the pressure that is on 
teenagers today, teenagers who are raised in what is, in fact, a seven-centered 
cultural environment.  In other words, the hangover of seven-centered philosophies, 
moralities, religions, all of that seven-centered strategic left, all of that stuff, this is 
the patina of the civilizations and cultures that we live in today. 
 
So our children, our parents, our grandparents, all of whom were born as nine-
centered beings spent, have spent, are spending their lives trying to be seven-
centered, trying to die young.  I guess that would be the best way of saying that, but 
would be a very dark joke.  Think about the Saturnian body.  All the pressure was on 
getting things done quickly, the kings and queens who were 16 and 17, the geniuses 
who were composing at 9, all this stuff.  There was this enormous pressure.   
 
Of course, the average peasant, because most of humanity was peasantry, at 15 was 
married and had children.  Think about that body.  Now, think about it from the 
deconstructed point of view.  The deconstructed point of view is that the body in 
itself, because it has the stamp that says till 30 everything is okay.  But the body 
itself is by its very nature only designed to function optimally within that 30-year 
span.  And in past generations, if you were a woman and you were over 20 and you 
had not had children there was something wrong with you.  If you were 30, it was a 
lost cause.  We look at that humorously today.  But of course, we don't have 
Saturnian bodies, nor are we under the Saturnian pressure of decay at 30.  It‟s 
decay.

The seven-centered being began to go downhill the moment they reached their 
Saturn return.  The nine-centered being coasts along on a roof.  There‟s no downhill.  
There is no deterioration until much, much later.  That is, our vehicles, these nine-
centered vehicles, when they came out of the factory they had a stamp that said 84 
years.  Think about that, because it's one of the most important things to grasp 
about our vehicles.   
 
And it‟s one of the most important things to keep in mind when you look at what is 
one of the most devastating and tragic phenomena on this planet.  We have all these 
people who are living forever.  I know.  I have a mother who in August, is going to 
97, and she is rare because she is not senile, and she is not crippled.  But all the 
human beings around her either die in their sleep or wake up without a memory.  
There is this vast pandemic of mental deterioration and physical deterioration that is 
taking place late in life.   
 
 
Quality of Life Increases as Resistance Decreases 
If we do not look after these vehicles in the way in which they were intended to be 
looked after, if we‟re caught in the seven-centered mechanism, seven-centered 
medicine, seven-centered homeopathy, seven-centered homogenized answers, then 
we‟re not going to be able to participate in the beauty of what this particular vehicle 
gives us, which is it gives us the potential of longevity and quality of life.  The quality 
of life is one of those of sound bites that gets bandied around a lot.  And within the 
context of how I am using this expression—quality of life—quality of life for me is 
very, very simple to understand.  That is, quality of life increases as resistance 
decreases.  This is a very simple formula: Quality of life increases as resistance 
decreases.   
 
Anybody who's ever been a part of the Human Design experiment and has entered 
into the experiment of Strategy and Authority, one of the things you find is that as 
you begin to navigate according to what is correct for you, you begin to reduce the 
resistance to what you are doing in the world.  You begin to reduce that resistance.  
You automatically feel better, because it's good for you. 
 
Variable 
There is something about the nine-centered vehicle that you will learn.  You will learn 
it next year.  It‟s perhaps the most complex level of this knowledge.  It's called 
Variable.  And what Variable teaches us is that the nine-centered being must be 
oriented correctly.  That means it has to operate correctly.  It has to eat correctly.  It 
has to be in the right place.  It has to be looking in the right direction in order for it 
to awaken as a being.

Introduction to External

This is the stage of transformation that is part of what you're being trained to do as 
a service to others.  The Center for Human Differentiation, which will open in 
September, is there to be able to take people beyond the entry level of their 
experiment.  And by saying „the entry-level‟, please understand that the entry level 
is the most important level, because without it there is nothing.  Without the 
experiment in Strategy and Authority there is no place to go.   
 
And remember something very clearly about what you're being trained for; you are 
not being trained to be an analyst.  A Human Design analyst is somebody who 
examines the surface, because that's where somebody who does not know needs to 
meet the truth.  This is not what you‟re being trained for.  You‟re being trained to 
take somebody, once that not-self surface has been shattered, who has entered into 
that experiment, to take them stage by stage through a process that is the 
enhancement of their potential.  
 
 
The Potential to Differentiate 
The first thing that you have learned in this process in terms of the internal is that 
you understand a basic ingredient in the transformation of a being.  Each and every 
one of us was born with the potential to differentiate.  Yet, differentiation is not 
something that has great power.  We already know that.  We come into the world, 
we‟re homogenized immediately.  We‟re homogenized by the way in which we are 
fed, the way in which we are treated, the way in which we are raised, the schools 
that we go to, the culture that we are a part of.  We have this deep, deep 
homogenization that takes place.   
 
It means that we have neural shut down.  That means that the brain and its neural 
expansion shuts down at approximately 3 years of age.  It means that our cognitive 
potential, our true cognitive potential is something that is submerged within a 
homogenized field.  And that its ability to be able to manifest itself is locked away by 
the fact that the conditioning is constantly being reinforced both by the diet and the 
behavior of the being. 
 
When somebody enters into their Strategy and Authority and they begin the process 
of making decisions through inner authority, this is what stops the deterioration.  
This puts a hold on the hooks of conditioning.  It does not remove them.  And as you 
well know anybody who enters into this process, and many of you are still in this 
stage, it is seven years.  It takes seven years of operating through what is your inner 
authority to be able to heal yourself of the conditioning.  And when I say heal 
yourself of the conditioning, it doesn't mean that the forces of conditioning have left 
you.  In healing them they begin to become a source of wisdom for you.

The Mind Cannot Be Healed by Correct Action 
But, the reality is that just simply operating according to your Strategy and 
Authority, though that transforms the life, and over a seven-year period is certainly 
going to remove the negative conditioning forces in your life.  It does not mean that 
in and of itself that is enough.  Most of the dilemma is mind.  And the dilemma with 
mind, with Personality, is that the Personality in and of itself cannot be healed by 
correct action.  Understand that.  You can be operating correctly in your experiment.  
Making decisions correctly doesn't mean that your mind has been healed.  It doesn't 
mean that the not-self mind that had so much power over you has really been 
stripped of its power.  It does not mean that it all.  
 
The reality is that there is no way in which you're going to ultimately be able to deal 
with that in mind unless you begin to feed the vehicle in a way that the potential for 
differentiation is nurtured.  It's a thing that makes the whole digestive process of the 
internal so absolutely incredible to really understand.  It‟s not just about digesting 
correctly so that in the physical sense you don't have indigestion, you don‟t have 
gas, you don‟t have this or that, you don‟t feel heavy, whatever the case may be, 
whatever the story is.  It isn‟t about that.  It‟s about what it means to follow your 
dietary regimen.  It means that everything you take in your body is used specifically 
to enhance your potential to be different.  That‟s what makes it so incredible.   
 
If you‟re not operating according to what it is correct for you in terms of your dietary 
regimen, you‟re following your Strategy and Authority, you have to understand that 
you are not helping yourself.  You're not enhancing that potential for differentiation 
without that enhancement, because that enhancement is all related to the brain 
system.  This is what you learn in PHS internal.  And we well know that the potential 
of the brain is what our Personality derives its mental potential from.  And if you 
think about the value of dietary regimen, it is enhancing those areas of brain 
function that are going to serve your cognition, and ultimately are going to pave the 
way for greater efficiency in terms of the way in which the Personality is going to be 
able to operate.  
 
The Nodes Align the Design with the Personality 
There's huge divide between the Sun/Earth over here 
and the Sun/Earth on the other side.  Oh, it‟s a chasm, 
it‟s a huge divide.  The only time we really see them in 
relationship to each other is when we see them in a 
cross.  But then again, it‟s just a cross.  You can see it 
even in the cross.  There is really a huge separation 
between these two.  Where the real miracle is is in the 
existence of a holistic being which comes from the two 
sets of Nodes.  It is the Nodes, it is the external that 
establishes not only the foundation for existence on this plane, but it is through the 
Nodes that we are actually able to align the Design with the Personality.

One of the things that you're going to learn at the beginning of next year in the first 
semester of the third year is one of the subjects called Link Nodes.  Again, it‟s very 
complex.  And yet, at the same time one of the most extraordinary things to grasp.  
There is a relationship between the 
Design Nodes and the Personality 
Nodes.  One of the things to 
understand about what happens to 
you 
when 
you're 
operating 
according to your Strategy and 
Authority because you are operating 
according 
to 
your 
vehicle's 
consciousness, that you're being 
aligned to the direction that is 
correct for you.  And the moment 
that you're operating according to 
your Strategy and Authority and 
you're aligning to your direction, 
that doesn't just impact the Design Nodes but also impacts the Personality Nodes on 
the other side and aligns them to this transition.   
 
The Nodes are linked to each other through the Magnetic Monopole.  And of course, 
we know it is the Magnetic Monopole that has two functions.  It holds us together in 
the illusion of our separateness.  The Sun/Earths are far apart but the Nodes bring 
us together through the agency of the Monopole that holds us together in the illusion 
of our separateness, but also gives us our direction, the nodal thematic of direction.  
Oh, it's a beautiful dance.  
 
 
Radical Steps in the Experiment 
The external; we‟re not talking about the brain anymore.  And there‟s something else 
to grasp.  The Sun/Earth, if you could hear the Sun/Earth, if they could speak, do

me, me, me, me, me, me, because this is what the Sun/Earth is.  It's all I, I, I, I, 
me, me, me.  It has nothing to do with anybody else.   
 
There are two radical steps that come with entering into the experiment.  The radical 
step of having your own bedroom, that‟s PHS internal.  The radical step of eating the 
way it is intended for you to eat as an individual in spite of what it means in terms of 
the social consequences.  These are two very, very radical steps, and they‟re deeply 
individual.  It‟s not about how my family should eat, so that you buy something that 
is there for the family to eat.  It‟s not the way it works.  It‟s I, I, what I need, what I 
need to put in my body in which way.

no, no, no, an apple a day keeps the doctor away.  Yeah, right.  Well, it‟s not true.

anywhere in the same location.  It can be anywhere.  That's why you have to be 
sensitive.  That‟s why you have to be correct; otherwise, you don't notice.

One Entrance 
Now, what‟s that cave?  The first thing about a cave is, well, the Chinese would call it 
feng shui.  The first thing is to recognize that there's one entrance.  This is the 
classic cave; one entrance.  And because this cave has one entrance, the entrance is 
defensible.  This goes back to our prehistory.  After all, human beings began by 
seeking security and shelter in caves.  And that cave with its one entrance gave 
everyone inside a sense of security.  It would be very uncomfortable to have a cave 
with two entrances.  There might be that secret escape road dug underneath 
somewhere, but the security in that is obvious as well.  And the entrance to it is 
hidden.   
 
The cave is that one opening that can be defended.  It‟s very, very important when 
dealing with particularly young children somewhere between three and seven years 
of age.  These are children who can begin to experience a lot of night fear.  Again, it 
would depend on their actual DreamRave and the kind of persona that they have, 
but the fact is that a lot of these children begin to experience uncomfortable dreams 
and begin to have problems with just night fears.  A lot of these children need a light 
on all night or these kinds of things.   
 
One of the things to understand is that if you have a child who has a PHS 1st Color

it means no en suite bathroom, just one door.  And as far as the closet is concerned, 
that has to be externalized in the sense of whatever, drawers and things and all that 
kind of stuff.  But it's about the door.

was not that there was Human Design or this or that.  The gift that was given here is 
the mechanics of what we are.  This is the mechanics of the nine-centered being so 
that we can realize our potential and leave behind the seven-centered malaise.  And 
it means that we need to move far beyond our first baby steps into correctness, this 
radical immersion in our potential for differentiation.   
 
I don't think anybody wants to wait 30 years, and I don't think it's necessary.  I just 
think that is the natural flow.  And I think it's only the natural flow for children who 
are raised correctly.  So, in fact, it's not a big question for us.  The gods have been 
kind to me.  I am shore person and I've been by the shore all my life.  I was born on 
an island in the middle of a river.  I've lived almost my entire life by the shore, my 
serendipity.  Not everybody has such serendipity.  This is quite a thing, this journey. 
 
Hardscape is Very Specific 
And to understand how important it is, because when we‟re dealing with the 
hardscape, just like when we are dealing with conditions, it is very, very specific.  
Enclosed space, safe, secure, enclosed space where you don't have to take in the 
outside frequency, the other‟s auras, the smells, anything, that you can be clear, 
that you can be safe.  And everything then operates according to your conditions.   
 
You're the one who can open the door or not and let somebody in or not.  It's your 
cave.  And you need that security.  You're never going to be that comfortable in 
somebody else's cave, even though it's a cave.  You like yours.  It‟s like that car that 
you love to drive and then you go away somewhere to do something and you have to 
rent a car, you‟re not really secure inside.  You need that sense of security.   
 
The cave says a great deal.  It says that this is the place where intelligence can be 
deeply nurtured.  Remember the resonance, the resonance to investigation to this, 
to that.  I know with my ten 1st lines, I spend most of my time in a cave, a cave by

that is deeply, deeply transformed and brought to your benefit when you're in the 
environment that‟s correct for you. 
 
Three Deeply Radical Stages 
You have to think about something.  There are three deeply radical stages that have 
to take place in Design and each of them brings their own dilemma.  And they bring 
a dilemma relative to the world around you. They just do.  You enter into your 
Strategy and Authority, and in entering into your Strategy and Authority you make 
decisions differently than you ever did before.  And you make decisions in a way that 
can be very difficult for those around you to grasp, appreciate, or want.  In that 
radical step you separate yourself from them when you sleep.  So, not only do you 
act differently in terms of how you decide, but you insist on having your own 
physical sleeping environment.  That‟s the first radical confrontation.

For me it all began within the context of understanding my own design.  I only have

makes them so interesting is how—I was going to say fussy, because I don't know 
another word for it.  We talk about the 2nd line as an archetype in the context of the 
hermit.  Unfortunately that name in many, many ways is misleading, though it's very 
true, but it's misleading.  That is, the assumption is that the 2nd line is always closed 
off.  And the thing to recognize about the 2nd line is that it‟s waiting for the call, 
which means that it can never be completely closed off, which means that it's open 
to something.

Ready for the Unknowable  
The moment we enter into the 2nd Color and we see the 2nd Color in terms of the 
Internal, the PHS Internal, what we see right away is that we have this opened and 
closed dietary regimen.  It is very important to see something about the 2.  If you go 
down to the tonal level what you get is this ability to be able to be ready for the 
unknowable.  You'll know it when you see it, but you don't know what the hell it is 
until you see it.  You know it when you see it, when you hear it, or smell it, or taste 
it, or whatever the case may be.  But the reality is that it is a capacity to be 
protected, in a way, more than just closed off.   
 
But at the same time, there is a probing.  Where this probing takes its full root, 
because we come from the bottom up from the Tone to the Color, is something you 
can really see here or at least something I enjoy looking at within this context, that 
is, looking at the 2nd Color phenomenon in terms of what that means 
environmentally.  The assumption environmentally, if you take it directly to the line 
level, is that you‟re closed off. 
 
What we know about the Nodes is exactly the opposite of that.  The Nodes represent 
not simply an environment for the benefit of the eye and its natural well-being, but it 
is an environment where one lives one's life and lives one's life in relation to the 
other.  So much about in the perfection of the other is something that is deeply, 
deeply rooted in the environment.  I guess you know that in a certain way, that kind 
of premise they have in romances where there's going to be that special moment, 
whatever it's going to be, and there's this whole thing about finding the right place, 
the right restaurant or the right location to set the environment for it to create 
exactly right environment in order to make that connection with the other more 
successful. 
 
It‟s just interesting the way the not-self distorts all of that.  What we get to see 
within the PHS External is environmentally the proper ground for us.  In other words, 
if the stage is set in that environment, then the stage is set for our interaction with 
the other.  Whether that interaction is going to be something that is casual or 
intimate or material or whatever the case may be, the reality is that it is that 
environment that is going to best nurture that.

The Relationship between Hot and Cold  
When you begin to think about deconstruction and Kitchens, and you think about all 
the things that go on in our modern life in terms of the way in which things are 
made, the way in which things are produced, the way in which things are 
researched, it goes on and on and on.  These are Kitchens.  This is one of those 
wonders. One of the most interesting things about the 3rd Color internal is this 
relationship between hot and cold.  We have been able to control heat for a very long 
time.  We‟re only beginning to understand how to control cold.  It is something that 
has emerged, obviously, only in the last hundred years.   
 
That is, if you go back to New York City in the 20s, you still had horse-drawn wagons 
carrying ice for people's ice boxes.  Cold is something we were grabbing from nature, 
but wasn't something we were creating ourselves.  One of the most extraordinary 
inventions of the last century was the ability to be able to create a refrigerator.  The 
refrigerator revolutionized the world.  It is an incredible thing.  We take it for granted 
now.  It's unbelievable how easy it is for people to take things for granted and not 
see what kind of unbelievable thing this is. 
 
I know the experience, as an example.  In my early years of living on the island 
where I didn't have a refrigerator, I didn't have electricity.  I didn‟t have any of those 
things.  It made your life much more complex.  You needed to be able to find food 
every day, whether that was being able to go someplace and get it and buy it, or 
whatever; but nonetheless, nothing would really stay very long.  It's a totally 
different life.   
 
I want you to grasp what Kitchens are all about.  I remember in the mid 90s in one 
of my earlier trips to the United States, I was invited to go to Las Vegas to teach a 
program.  I arrived there and the temperature was about 110°, 41, 42, 43 Celsius, 
somewhere in that neighborhood.  It was very, very, obviously incredibly hot.  This is 
a city that is built in the middle of the desert.  It has no natural water of its own.  All 
its waters are imported from afar.  Here you are in—I got lost because I suddenly 
had a flash about something that happened to me there, but it was so amazing that 
there were people there.  There were 500-, 600-, 700-, 800,000 people who were 
living there.  No human being ever, ever, ever, ever, ever could have lived there.  No 
native tribes lived there.  
 
You come out of Death Valley and the first thing you see is the lights of Vegas.  What 
changed that?  The Kitchens, air-conditioning, the cold.  Oh there are so many places 
in the world where people now can function a totally different way.  If you have to 
live in a climate where the temperature is 100° and you don't have air-conditioning, 
you're not going to be doing a lot, because you can‟t.  It‟s always astonishing to me 
that human beings don't, aren't aware of the most obvious things.  Hot and cold is

The Need for Reduction in Oxygen  
So, Mountains.  The first thing that's interesting about Mountain and something that, 
because it was part of one of your earlier exams and you all dealt with this rather 
well, Mountain is deeply connected to oxygen levels.  Now, oxygen is an interesting 
thing.  After all, oxygen is corrosive.  It really is fire, in a way.  It is what ignites our 
life.  After all, it is the oxygen that sets our life on fire.  It‟s very interesting about 
the brain and its relationship to oxygen.  If you overload the brain with oxygen, for 
example, what the abuse of alcohol does, you deteriorate brain function.  If you 
reduced oxygen in the brain, what Aldous Huxley discovered, is that you alter 
perception.  Well, not just Aldous Huxley, any high-level Mountain climber knows 
that the lack of oxygen can lead to hallucinations and can lead to all kinds of mental 
phenomena.  It is not surprising that the great meditative mystics in the East love 
the high ground, because a high ground, that reducing of oxygen in the brain, leads 
to the stimulation of the brain and to potentials of altered perception.   
 
One of the things, for example, what Huxley was experimenting with which was 
mescaline and lysergic acid.  LSD does the same thing.  These are oxygen strippers.  
They take oxygen out of the brain and in taking oxygen out of the brain, induce an 
altered state.  It‟s one of the reasons that there are in the world, I assume, about 6 
or 700 million people who are addicted to tobacco.  Tobacco, because of its impact 
on of the cilia in the bronchial tubes, that is, they're sort of like little fingers like 
underneath your vacuum cleaner, these little fingers that are constantly moving and 
basically they're cleaning.  What happens as you smoke is that they begin to get 
covered in tar and they no longer can do the same proper cleaning, which means 
that less oxygen is getting into the of the body and as a result less oxygen is getting 
into the brain.

One of the most interesting things about the tobacco question and again, I may be

that there is no question that there are certain carcinogenic properties in tobacco.  
But then again, those things really don't mean anything to me in the sense that it is 
about the homogenous vehicle, so, the fact that there is a generic source of this or 
that simply means that the homogenization is created a lot of similarity that leads to 
problems. 
 
What I have noticed over the years, though, is that you rarely get to see the positive 
side of the impact of nicotine.  And there have been some deeply valuable scientific 
and scholarly works on the values of nicotine that simply never get the light of day, 
because it is overwhelmed by the anti-smoking, anti-tobacco lobby.  By the way, I‟m 
not lobbying for smoking.  I'm not a generalist.  Everybody operates according to 
their authority.  It‟s just one of those things that is interesting, is one of the most 
interesting phenomena of all tribes, is how smoking is something that is basically a 
global common tradition regardless of what they were smoking.

did a worldwide survey of those who are addicted to tobacco, we would come up with 
a disproportionate number of those that have 4th Color external, because that is one 
of the things, this reduction in oxygen.

make them healthier.  God, I love how the heretical ridiculous is so delicious, isn‟t it?

Mountain and the need for this reduction in oxygen.

nothing to look at.  That high ground makes a difference.

simply sets the platform for the way in which you can live a life that has to be one 
that you meet.  In other words, all of that is fine and good, to be ready to make a 
decision correctly, to nourish oneself correctly, but one has to be in the world.

is not location.  It‟s not a place, in that sense.  It‟s not a specific place.  You can find

in these 21 years now, that it was all just a gradual process limited by the nature of 
cellular regeneration, at least a minimum of seven years in the sense of the process.

Hardscape and Landscape

ultimately became the first true cities.   
 
They emerged in Mesopotamia, probably somewhere between 10 to 15,000 years 
ago, villages that would surround themselves with some kind of protective barrier.

for a community to cohere together for hierarchies to establish for the basic

The Nodes  
It‟s through the Nodes that the life is structured.  This is what you‟ll come to deal 
with more often next year.  That is Variable and what this Independent Variable is.  
It is so important to grasp that it establishes the foundation for the life and its 
movement.  It is what makes it possible to live out the full potential of this Uranian 
body.   
 
The fact that the real maturation of a Uranian body is post-Kiron means that there is 
a demand for well-being in this form.  It's a demand.  We know the deterioration 
that can take place physically in this life.  And for most of our history the idea that 
life was going to be at its fullest between 50 and 84 would have seemed like a cruel 
joke.  The Uranian body, simply because it exists and simply because it has a 
capacity for extended wear and tear, doesn't mean that it's optimized if you just let 
go of it.  We do is see that there is a huge expansion in the aged of the world.  There 
are millions right now of people in the United States, for example, who are over 100.  
My mother is about to turn 97.  You can see that there is this huge thing.  
 
And yet, at the same time, and my mother is a classic example of that.  She‟s very 
fortunate in that she is physically and mentally well.  She‟s just very old.  She lives 
in one of these environments where she has a private space, but there are doctors, 
nurses, these kinds of things that are available, sort of like a community for very old 
people.  90% of them are incapacitated either physically or mentally.  She had the 
experience now over the last 10 years of sharing a lunch with somebody and having 
a conversation, and the next day, worse than being dead, suddenly it's just gone.  
The onset of Alzheimer‟s, they slip and fall down, or whatever the case may be.   
 
She's bright woman and she looks out at all of that and one of her questions, it‟s not 
even a question, it is just a rhetorical sort of statement.  She says she can't quite 
see what the purpose is in having such longevity.  She's at a point now where she‟s 
only comfortable reading for maybe a half hour at the time.  Her larynx is so old that 
the food, everything has to be soft.  
 
One of the things to keep in mind about the Uranian vehicle is that even if you're 
blessed with good genes, the reality is that it is quite a thing to be able to fulfill one's 
life and the quality of life in these vehicles.  And not to be alone when you get into 
the latter part of this process, because everybody else in their dysfunction and in 
their not-self reality dissolves slowly but surely.  Most human beings on the planet 
now, in the West anyway, the cancer and heart disease statistics don't really make 
sense because most of them are related to people between 70 and 90.  And these 
are Uranian vehicles that are just collapsing.

Everything about the Nodes is that they provide us with something that's absolutely 
essential to grasp.  There is an environment that nurtures our particular unique 
form.  Obviously, it‟s a different nurturing than takes place in the digestive process

The Valley Brings the Strangers

these tribal societies.  That is, the foreigners, the strangers, the people with different 
habits, though there are some that are very attracted to that and find it stimulating

this is what‟s interesting, but it‟s dangerous.

can see the danger.  We can see the danger that that brings.  We can see what that

South Africa and the South Africans went crazy.  They just started to kill them and 
push them out.  You don't have to go there; there are so many different places in 
the world where there are examples of that where the stranger is not welcome.

them for the first time.  And in that, being able to understand some very, very

Aligning to your Environment is Key to Completing the Design Process  
And it‟s not a place.  Get that out of your head.  It‟s the same thing with PHS 
internal.  Everybody you‟re going to introduce PHS internal is going to think it‟s 
about food.  It‟s not about food.  It‟s about how you eat.  It‟s not about food.  This 
isn‟t about place.  It's about the environment.  It's the environment.  The moment 
you're aligned to that environment is the key to completing your process on the

We have this transformation process.  You start with a catalyst of Strategy and 
Authority.  You enter into your PHS internal and you begin the process of enhancing 
what are the basic differentiated traits that you have.  And the step that follows, the 
step that completes the process is the environmental step.  By the way, it doesn't 
mean you have to be in your environment 24 hours a day.  It doesn't.  But it does 
mean that a great part of your life should be connected to your environment.  That is

somebody, it needs to be in your environment.  It has to be correct for you.  
Communication for the Valley person is what the environment is all about.   
 
Environment and the Saturn Return 
Everything you have to do in Human Design to get to awareness, really perfected 
awareness, is just one radical step after another.  That‟s what it is.  Environment is 
something you can be fortunate with or not in terms of the relationships you have 
and the way you've been living your life.  Above all else, you can be doing your PHS 
internal, but the longer you avoid environment—again, remember something about 
environment.  I want to reiterate this because it's so important to keep this in mind.  
Environment is something that should naturally be achieved when your reach your 
Saturn return.  A child raised correctly in all ways, when they reach their Saturn 
return, that‟s the time for them to go out into the world and secure their place in 
their environment.   
 
Environment is a Healing Field

way.  We did not come into the world correct.  We did not.  And it means that 
environment is something that has been lacking in our lives, potentially or not.  
Some of us have serendipity in that sense, and it is something to see that when 
you're ready, when it's correct for you, then it's not something to wait for.  It‟s long 
overdue.  It's an important ingredient.  It‟s long overdue in the sense of the long-
term viability of your form, the long-term well-being of this Uranian vehicle.  And for 
all of us who have been deconditioned, we are dealing with the deterioration that 
took place during our not-self lives, and we‟re still dealing with the consequences 
that arose out of that.

Nodal Color 6: Shores 
 
 
 
Hello and welcome to all of.  We come to the end of our nodal Color journey.  It is 
always interesting what happens when you get to the 6s.  I guess that when I think 
about it myself actually, I think that probably Zen has it best when it comes to 
describing the nature of a 6.  That is, the expression nedi, nedi; not this, not that.  
This is the idealized or perfected state of the 6.  That is, it is not this, it is not that. 
 
Nedi, Nedi  
When we began the class today we were talking briefly about 6th line beings and the 
tripartite life process.  When you think about that process, when you think about the 
three parts of that life you have the subjective and you have the objective, so you 
have the subjective beginning, the Saturnian cycle, you have the objective phase on 
the roof, and then you have something else.  That something else is nedi, nedi.  That 
is, it‟s not this and it‟s not that.   
 
I like to think of it as aliveness.  I think that's the easiest way to think about it as 
opposed to putting any intent on what the aliveness is for.  The thing about the 6 is 
just „being‟ rather than anything else.  One of the dilemmas you find in this is that 
it‟s very difficult to reach such a stage.  That is, this is the mutation that's moving us 
forward.  The 6th line and the way the 6th line is keynoted, this is a role model.  This 
is the way in which it‟s supposed to be.  In a differentiated world, each being is a 
perfected example of themselves, whatever that may be and whatever the by-
product of that perfected example of themselves may be. 
 
The 6: A Place of Transition and a Boundary 
Also for me, every time that I look at any of the configurations, whether tonal, Color, 
line, that when you're looking at this movement to the 6 you‟re seeing a place of 
transition.  You‟re seeing a place that no longer is deeply connected to the processes 
that have come before it.  And in a sense, this is very true for the nature of Shores.  
Shores is a very odd thing in a way.  There is certainly the obvious of it.  I‟m a 
classic example of that.  Both my Design and Personality Nodes are 6th Color and I 
was born on an island.  I think I‟ve lived at least 95% of my life on islands or by 
seashores.  I can remember as a child, we had a lakeshore house as a summer 
house and my father was somebody who loved the ocean.  Every summer was spent 
by the seashore, which were always the best times of the year for me.

There is the physical example of Shores.  And obviously my having this experience

of an obvious serendipity for me in terms of the way in which my life has worked.  
So there's this obvious you can see it in the tonal influence, there is an obvious 
natural quality of the Shores.  There is also an obvious artificial value to Shores.  
Basically the Shores are a boundary.  It is a boundary line that is ideally beyond the 
“this and that.”  It‟s not necessarily a boundary line between this and that.   
 
It's like going down to the shore in a sense.  You go down to the shore and what 
does the shore represent?  In one way it's a limitation.  To live on an island, as an 
example, island people will always say the same thing that there's a certain 
imprisonment, there is a certain locking in.  It‟s one of the things that is part of that.  
You‟re there on the shore and then there is this vast whatever on the other side.  
And there is a dividing line, but the dividing line isn't so that you are particularly in 
the right environment if you're on one side of it, it has a great deal to do with the 
way in which that boundary impacts the way in which you‟re moved in this life.  I 
can't really find another word for that. 
 
One of the things so beautiful about the seashore an example is it‟s not so much the 
seashore itself and playing in the sand, and so forth and so on and all that stuff.  It 
is about the far-off distant horizon that is there, this other world that could be out 
there, the beyond that‟s out there that sets the imagination going.  Boundaries are 
like that. And these boundaries are about a fascination with what's on the other side 
without necessarily going there.   
 
The Environment Sets You Up for the Way You See  
Remember something about the Nodes.  It's something that you will learn really 
much more about next year, but it's important for you to begin to think this way 
about the Nodes, how deeply connected they are to each other.  That is, Personality 
and Design Nodes.  This is literally laying out the foundation upon which your whole 
life process is going to work.  And one of things to recognize about the Shore is that 
through we‟re talking about a physical environment, remember that we've been 
using other kinds of metaphors, you're a Mountain person and you take the high 
ground, you‟re a Valley person and you‟re down there on the ground floor.  There are 
different ways of metaphorically looking at these things. 
 
Shores: Seeing Something Beyond  
One of things to grasp about the Shore is that it is opening up the possibility for the 
prospective to these broad horizons. Remember that your environment sets you up 
for the way you‟re going to see.  So, if you're in a Cave, you‟re set up to see the 
walls, are you not?  And if you‟re on the Shore, you‟re set up to see something 
beyond, something that's the other side, something that‟s somewhere else,

Looking Out Over the Horizon  
In other words, there are many, many, many, many kinds of Shores.  The thing to 
understand about the Shores is that it‟s setting up a different kind of perspective and

The Interest is what They are on the Edge Of

you‟re on the boundary of.  That's what matters.  The interesting thing about a 
Shore is that it can be high; it can be low, particularly when you get into any kind of 
artificial demarcation in these kinds of boundaries.  They can exist anywhere. 
 
So, it isn‟t so much that the environment is important in terms of where the person 
is; it's important in terms of what they are on the edge of.  They can be on the edge 
of something that they don‟t like—someone in the suburbs who doesn‟t like the city 
and lives outside of that.  This is what is going to set up the way in which it‟s going

And then you get to the 6.  This is the exact furthest polarity to the Cave.  It's wide

The Shore: Being Available

so interesting about being on the Shore.  Being on the Shore is being available.  It's 
also not being available, but it‟s there.  It‟s like the 19th gate, 6th line, the fool on the 
hill.  It‟s there.  It doesn‟t mean that it‟s necessarily approached or not, but it's

potential conditioning.  There are a lot less social restrictions.  You notice that by the 
shore, don‟t you, wherever you look at the shore?  You see human beings take on a 
very, very different kind of behavioral pattern.   
 
It is a huge difference to be on the outside of something.  For example, I live in the 
countryside not too far away from a village.  And these are two archetypes of life: 
country life/village life.  The people who live in the village are very, very different 
from the people who don't live in the village.  And living on the boundary of the 
village, because I live on the boundary of the village, it is one of the things that I get 
to see.  I get to see how different they are.  At the same time, I get to interact with 
them, because this is the whole thing about being on the boundary.  The whole thing 
about Shores is that you can get into your little boat and start paddling.  It‟s the 
whole thing about the boundary.  You can go to the other side and come back.  It's 
about being on that jumping off point.  It's about being at that vantage point.   
 
The 6 is a Deeply Important Place of Transition  
And it's about starting something new.  Everything about the nature of the 6th line is 
about transition.  You look at the structure of the hexagram as it‟s laid out in the 
wheel and you get to the 6th line and the next line that follows is a 1.  You look at 
profiles and you get a 6/3 and the next profile is a 1/3.  The 6 is a deeply, deeply 
important place of transition.  It‟s always moving towards that transition.  So being 
on the Shore is being ready for that transition.  It makes it possible.

What happens is that all the shorelines, because the shorelines are endless in that 
sense, this becomes the environmental necessity.  If you have somebody who has a 
6th Color environment, it is probably the easiest of all of the environments to 
deconstruct, because there are so many boundaries you can see, both natural and 
artificial, so many boundaries you can find in any given place, literally in any given 
place.  They‟re just there.    
 
The dilemma is not so much being able to find, or being able to express to the 6th 
Color being what is a suitable deconstructed environment.  It isn't about that.  The 
thing that's important for them is what's on the other side of that boundary, because 
that's what's going to determine in so many ways the quality of their life.  If one is 
correct, obviously, and one is operating through one‟s Strategy and Authority, you 
are going to be able to get to what is that specific boundary that is necessary for 
you. 
 
It‟s like moving from downtown to uptown or whatever the case maybe.  It may be 
the most mundane thing, and yet—view, the thing that makes the landscape so 
interesting is that all of landscape is about the view.  There is no view in the 
hardscape.  Not really.  That is, everything is focused on what is enclosed rather 
than the openness that is out there in the context of landscape.  Everything about 
landscape is its view.  We saw that in the Mountain people, as an example, where 
they have a different perspective because they have the view from the high ground, 
in the same way that the 5th Color beings have a different perspective, the Valley 
people have a different perspective.  Their advantage is that they see things from 
the ground floor.  They have a view in which they see the movement of things.  This 
is part of it. 
 
The 6th Color Needs a View in Front of Them  
The thing about the 6 is the view on the world that they have.  They need to have a 
view in front of them.  That boundary is for them to look on to something, or into 
something, or over to something.  And they have to be on the other side looking at 
it.  This is when the environment is perfect for them.  This is when it‟s just perfect.  
You have a valley, you have a river running through the valley, which side of the 
valley—it sounds like a chicken and egg story—but to be on the right side looking in 
the right direction is what it's all about, because everything for the Shore being is 
what is the—view is not really even the word—it has to have a higher value.  It‟s the 
horizon.  What is horizon out there?  What is that thing you can look out on from 
your boundary point that you're not of, but that you're drawn to, that it is interesting 
and fascinating and whatever the case may be.  
 
Of course, none of this has anything to do with the speculations of mind.  All of it 
would be a natural by-product of correctness.  Here with Shores, I think it‟s very 
difficult for somebody who's recently deconditioned and recently entered into their 
Strategy and Authority to really be able to find the correct environment for them.  I 
think there's much more work to do with the physical body before you can really get

Each Environment Affects the Consciousness  
It‟s very interesting to think about this environmentally, what environments bring 
into our consciousness, each of these environments.  And there's no good or bad, 
and there's this one is better than that one, and all that stuff.  It‟s not about that.  
It‟s about understanding that each of these environments affects the consciousness.  
They have to.  And because there is this intrinsic relationship between the Nodes, 
that is, through the Link Nodes, you have to see that environment is always 
automatically connected to the way in which your perspective is going to operate, 
what in Rave Psychology is referred to as the View. 
 
The reality is that what you're looking at here is the environmental framing for the 
way in which Personality consciousness is going to take in the world, this world that's 
provided by this environment.  So, each and every one of these environments lead 
to a change in the consciousness.  You can see that in Kitchens, for example, where 
suddenly in the consciousness is the understanding that basic elements can be 
transformed.  It leads to what ultimately would be the industrial age, leads to our 
ability to be able to create things out of their basic elements, and all of that through 
the agency of the control of heat and cold, and so forth and so on.  It changes the 
consciousness. 
 
So, when you're looking in an environment, don't just look at it as just simply as a 
kind of an environmental location, thinking of it as a certain place to find, and so 
forth and so on.  You have to take this deeper.  This quality of life, the health and 
well-being, the bringing down of the level of resistance, opening up the opportunity 
for the correct interaction with others, this is all part of what the Nodes bring.  It‟s 
very, very powerful.  And at the same time to see that all of that is framing, it‟s in 
the vehicle after all, all of this is framing the way in which ultimately the Personality 
is going to interpret experience.   
 
So, the moment we get to the Shores in this evolutionary movement is the moment 
we get to this place of intellectual duality, I guess, where you begin to see that in 
the vastness of everything is the “this and that.”  It‟s huge.  It becomes enormous.  
And it means the potential of the consciousness is one that's going to go beyond the 
mundane.  We know if you're looking at these numbers in terms of their line values, 
for example, if you look at the 5th line and its universalization, there is an inherent 
drive to express that and make sure that everybody gets it.  But that's not true in 
the 6.  It doesn‟t work in that way.  It is not a honed or specific message, because 
that's what the Valley does.  It creates is honed message that can travel all over. 
 
But it also is a limitation in terms of the way in which the consciousness can work.  
The consciousness will be streamed and focused by the Valley.  But when you‟re 
dealing with the Shore you‟re dealing with something else.  You‟re dealing with a 
whole different phenomenon that is beyond you, that you're ready to take in, which 
means you have to be competent to be able to take things in at that level.  In other 
words, take in that spectrum. It‟s the expansion of consciousness.  And it is a 
transition that in our sense clearly is a transition that speaks for what it is to be a 
nine-centered being, what it is to be us.

Tone Fixes the Color Resonance

the Design side, the relationship inherently between Tone and Color is fundamentally 
different than the relationship on the Personality side.  On the Design side what 
always happens in correctness, and please remember that I'm talking specifically 
about the theoretical of being correct, that the moment that one is correct is the 
moment that the Tone is going to fix the Color.  This is the whole thing about 
understanding the Tonal/Color relationship on the Design side, whether you're 
looking at the internal or the external.  The moment there is correctness is the 
moment the being is operating according to Strategy and Authority is the moment 
that the Tone fixes the Color.

and that” of the left and right-ness.  And here it fixes the resonance.  Again, 
remember it's fixing the resonance; it‟s not fixing anything else.  In other words, it is 
locking into one particular attraction to a particular environment.  A particular 
attraction to, let's call it a quality of that environment, because basically when you're 
looking at the left-ness and the right-ness in the external you're looking at a

Leftness and Rightness Make Up a Variable

differences that are part of what is the most revealing aspect of the work of PHS and

the Design Sun/Earth and Nodes, and the Personality Sun/Earth and Nodes are 
oriented according to left-ness and right-ness.  Each and every one of these of these 
four aspects of Variable is what I refer to as the four ways of transformation.  In 
other words, each of them represents a fundamental transformation that is 
necessary in order for the full process that is the completion of differentiation to take 
place in the being.

Every Step Alienates You to the Not-Self  
We've already seen in the deconstruction of external, we can use it in many ways.  
We know if the person is ill and they‟re a 1, we know what to do with them.  We 
know what to do with being able to take advantage at the superficial level of what 
environment is all about.  It's about a step-by-step process, because there is a great 
dilemma that is attached with transformation and most people ignore it.  And it can't 
be ignored.  If you‟re going to be transformed, if you‟re going to go from Strategy 
and Authority to each of the transformations, every single step along the way 
alienates you to the other, every step along the way.  And when I say alienates you 
to the other, obviously I'm speaking of the not-self, but it‟s true. 
 
You begin by saying, “I‟m going to make decisions this way.  If you don‟t like it, what 
am I going to do, I have to make decisions according to my Strategy and Authority.”  
That‟s resistance number one to the not-self world around you, so you go through 
that.  Then you enter into dietary regimen.  Dietary regimen is a radical, radical 
challenge to the way in which we‟ve lived our lives for everyone.  For everyone 
including myself it is a radical challenge to live your PHS.  You have to deal with the 
way in which food has been integrated into our lives and how complex it is for us to 
change our dietary pattern.  And yet at the same time, like everything in Design, it 
doesn‟t take a rocket scientist to follow your PHS.  It is basically just a simple thing.   
 
It isn't simple in the consequences socially.  It isn't simple in the resistance that 
arises out of that relative to the not-self.  It is an alienating process.  Remember, 
Human Design is about getting you to be your own authority.  The moment you get 
to be your own authority, you‟re a force in this world that is unlike anything that is 
around you, because everybody else is guided by everything else and as you're 
moving towards that, you must understand this is something that deals with a 
tremendous amount of social pressure.  And social pressure is what the mind will 
play with and the mind is the last thing to heal, the mind trying to deal with its old 
discomforts, vis-à-vis the way in which it is related to by others.

environment.  This is something that is really important.

Environment Brings Out a Direction of Activity  
The other thing is that the tonal works at many, many levels.  There seems to be an 
obvious of it.  For example, let‟s take the Wet, that one resonates to a humid 
environment or resonating to taking a lot of showers or resonating to being a 
swimmer, all of these kinds of things that have to do not just simply with the quality 
but also what that leads to in terms of activity in life.  Because remember that 
whenever you're dealing with the Nodes, you're not just dealing with a specific 
environment.  You have to remember something: every environment creates a way 
in which human beings interact, the way they do things.  You don't have the same 
kind of activity in a hot place as you do in a dry place, a high place or a low place.  
In other words, the moment you have an environment, you also have an impact.  It 
brings out a direction, deconstructed in many cases, a direction of activity.   
 
So for example, if you're somebody who resonates to the wet then you can see that 
will lead to certain kinds of activity or certain kinds of places that you enjoy being.  
And it leads to different kinds of connections.  For example, let's say that you‟re into 
swimming.  Now, the reality is that if you're into swimming and you're going to go to 
the wet place, in that wet place you‟re going to meet people who, like yourself, are 
also interested in whatever that is.  In another words, they are the people that have

different people on the south side and the north side.  They‟re going to be different.

Environment is a Stage 
The very environment creates the difference.  Let‟s say the south side is the poor 
side and the north side is the rich side, and the line of tracks runs between the 
middle or whatever the case may be.  It‟s something to grasp about environment.  
Environment isn‟t the weather, and it isn't a place.  It's the complete staging, it's a 
stage.  And it‟s a stage that is dressed accordingly.  It‟s a stage that has this or that 
to it.  And that stage has human beings as a part of it.  These are the extras, this is 
what crowds the world, all those beings you don‟t know, the crowd, the world that 
plays out the background of your life.  And you walk down that street.  
 
It‟s more than just a busy place, a factory that‟s humid inside.  There are people 
working, there are places they go.  There is an area around it.  There are all of these 
things that come with the environment, all of it, and each of these aspects of the 
environment changes.  There‟s a big difference between the service industrial 
complex that we have developed in our hardscape that we get to the 3rd Color, the 
difference between the wet and the dry.  It leads to different kinds of people in 
different kinds of situations doing different kinds of things. 
 
It‟s your environment that aligns you to those beings who are kindred.  They are not 
unintentional strangers.  That‟s the other thing that is so interesting about 
understanding the nature of order.  When you get to look at the Variable plate, there 
are 16 different zones.  You stand in any one of those zones and you look around 
you.  It is the way it is.  That is, we are all here in the perfection of finding the 
completion for us.  We are all here to express what is a unique perception, but a 
unique perception that is only possible not simply when we can just make that 
unique expression, but then when there are those who are aligned and ready for that 
expression to receive it.   
 
The environment is the place where you live out your life, where you live out the 
Uranian life to its fullest.  It‟s going to make a big difference whether you are going 
to be on the wet side or the dry side.  Let's say that you're for the dry and you end 
up in the wet.  You meet the wrong people, don‟t you?  You‟re not in the right place 
anymore.  The environment is not correct for you.  And you‟re constantly going to be 
having problems with it, constantly.  And it‟s going to lead to different things.  It can 
lead to physical problems, it can lead to psychological problems, and it can lead to all 
kinds of things.  But the thing that it leads to deepest is that you are not communing 
with those beings who are there on your fractal to truly receive what you can offer.

Environment is very profound.  That's why it's so important for those who are trained 
in this knowledge to understand the deconstructed values, because everybody can 
have access to their environment at one level or another.  It was not like you have to 
give up your life and move to Mt. Everest, you don't.  But more and more I 
understand how profound it is as a nurturing, healing mechanism for the life.  Not 
the body in that sense directly, but the life itself.  Because of course there is an

a mutation in the visual cortex, it is a mutation that is opening up the possibility of 
the arrival of Solar Plexus awareness and it is opening up an entirely different way in 
which the processing of experience takes place.   
 
Genetic Markers for Autism  
I shared a recent study that I had just been investigating with Andrea the other day 
and it was a study into autism genetics, the latest study out of Harvard.  It‟s quite a 
detailed study and very interesting.  One of the things they have finally come to 
recognize about it is that every single one of the genetic markers for autism, and 
there are many by the way, is peculiarly differentiated.  But of the genetic markers 
for autism they have established, what they do is interfere with the way in which the 
brain stores experience in the form.  That is, basically the way in which neurons fire 
and the way in which synapse work.  And of course, what is so fascinating is that we 
have the advent of Solar Plexus awareness.  We have the advent of what is in fact a 
neural capacity that physically, at least as a measurement, exceeds what we now 
have in terms of our neo-cortex.  So the Solar Plexus as it‟s understood, even today 
by science, and it‟s still rudimentary, is that they're astonished to establish such 
neural capacity in the Solar Plexus.

Transforming Your Frequency through the Vehicle  
It‟s all about the Monopole.  This is the great magic of Human Design, the Monopole.  
It captures frequency.  So everything we understand, ultimately, is simply about

in the 60s about people having a vibe, a vibration, good or bad vibrations, this sense 
of the recognition of frequency.  And it's something to understand about what it is to 
be correct is that it is the refinement of that.  And you don't refine your frequency 
with your mind.  That‟s the sad black joke that the homogenized world gets into.  
You transform your frequency through the vehicle.  And that transformation of 
frequency is a fine tuning process.  It's quite something to really be in tune.  It's 
always interesting for me when I tune my guitar with my ear, and I have a very 
good ear, and then I'll match it against the an electronic tuning, and I'm always 
slightly sharp or flat.  It‟s never exactly on.  Now, nobody but somebody with a most 
peculiar hearing would even notice that.  And yet, it's slightly off.

transformations that a human being can go through is a refinement of the frequency.

which the body receives nutrition.  It's the first thing.  And it's something that you

PHS.  It is what is natural for them.  It is what is natural for them from day one.  It‟s

context—they don't have a four transformation process as we do, they have two.  
That‟s all.  They‟re raised according to their nature.  They are nourished according to 
their nature.  That's not a transformation.  That's their nature.  And then they have 
to find their environment so they can ultimately release their outer authority.

transformations at all.  There‟s only two.  But for us, there are four.  It begins with

It‟ll end up being a horrible charade for you and it won‟t be something that is worthy 
of this process.  It has to be correct.  If it's correct for you to enter into your PHS,

wrong with you.  It just means that there is another timing.  Everybody has timing 
for everything.  And timings are unique.  You cannot get involved in comparing.

Standing Alone  
The one thing about Human Design is that this is not about instant gratification.  One 
of the most of basic time frames in Design is the seven-year cycle that people 
coming in are told, that this is a process.  One of the things about what it means to 
embrace this knowledge is, yes, it's going to change your life, yes, it‟s going to 
change your awareness, and it is something to see that it is going to change the way 
you see the world around you, the way the world around you sees you and you‟re 
going to have to deal with that for the first time, what it means truly to stand alone, 
and to stand alone within the context of your authority.   
 
We are in the conditioned being deconditioned.  And this is an enormous struggle.  
I'm not a fool.  I know how powerful the homogenized world is.  It is unbelievably 
powerful.  Whether you're awake or you're asleep, the homogenized world is having 
a deep and profound influence on you because we are nothing but receptors.  This is 
what we're all about.  We have this little bit of activation and a lot of receptors.  And 
until we lean on those activations, only then can we get this because remember, its 
resonance, and you don't resonate to what's good for you through your openness.  
You resonate to what's good for you through your activation, who in fact you 
are.  That‟s the thing that‟s so incredible. 
 
This is the beauty of this process.  The seven-centered being was ruled by fear.  
Everything about them was a way to deal with fears.  Everything about what they 
needed to know, the way they would act, the way the world was built, all of this has 
been built on fear, and it is a fear that is rooted in the fundamental that is survival.  
If you really want to see the evidence of seven-centered consciousness in the world 
today, then look at the billions who are on the edge of survival.  The deep, deep 
failure of this process in which you see so many billions of human beings, billions and 
billions of human beings who are suffering.  It‟s quite a world out there, a world in 
which the lack of correctness causes so much pain and it causes so much suffering.   
 
Seven-Centered Fear  
All we‟re here to do is to be able to give up on what that seven-centered fear did to 
us and has left as its mark.  The brain isn‟t about fear.  The mind is conditioned to be 
fearful.  It‟s the seven-centered business.  Now, the moment the mind is no longer 
your authority, it no longer has the power to distort you through that fear that has 
homogenized you and this planet.  There's only one way to get beyond that fear is 
when you transfer the authority away from the Personality and you give the 
authority to the Design.  Your vehicle in making decisions for itself; vehicles make 
perfect decisions.  They know precisely what is necessary, precisely.  It‟s a Uranian 
vehicle.  It‟s here to live out a very healthy, fruitful life, far different from anything 
the seven-centered being knew.  And without fear of what should I do, what must I

ix 
 
The IHDS PHS Certification Year 3: PHS Practicioner  
 
The Primary Health System (PHS) is a revolutionary approach to well being. PHS was 
first introduced by Ra Uru Hu in April 2003. As empowering as Human Design is for 
the Personality Passenger, PHS is the awakening key for the Design Vehicle.  
 
PHS is the result of years of ground laying work in the sub-structure of the Line. The 
PHS Practitioner Program Year Two focuses on the 216 Chains that define our 
possible paths of differentiation and well being. 
 
The PHS Primary Health System Analyst program is open to any student who has 
completed PHS Analyst Year One program.  
 
The IHDS is the official Human Design and Global Incarnation Index certifying and 
licensing authority. The IHDS offers programs leading to official certification in both 
teaching and practice.  
 
The PHS Primary Health System Practitioner is such a credit program. To complete 
this program for certification, students are required to complete the entire program 
of three years.  
 
This book is a transcript of the third year, first semester originally taught in Fall of 
2007. 
 
~

Well here we are. I must say I am unusually excited about the prospect of going 
through both this program and the following psychology program at this level. I'd 
like to make a comment to all of you of the time and the investment that you have 
made and the dedication that you've brought to this program.  I have mentioned it 
before; I mentioned it on the radio not too long ago that you really are best 
educated relative to any program that's ever been offered in Human Design.  It is 
something that for me in an odd selfish sort of way provides me with one of the most 
exciting opportunities I have as a teacher of this knowledge is to take the knowledge 
to another level, to another level of perception.  None of that would have been 
possible without the serendipity of many things—this kind of technology, the 
involvement of students in a long-term process in which quite frankly none of us 
really knew where we were going.  All of this is very exciting.  And the knowledge 
even at its basic level relative to PHS is already quite remarkable.  But, you will have 
a taste of that today because I will show you a whole new way of seeing in that 
sense.

The Potential for Perfection

the perspective of PHS, is that we see that our bodies are potentially designed to be

in terms of being able to live uniqueness that there are certain basic criteria.  One is 
that you operate out of the intelligence of the form, that it is form intelligence that 
guides you, whatever that authority happens to be.

intelligence of the form doesn't make up for years and years and years of 
conditioning.  Nor does it give the greatest possible advantage to the manifestation 
or the realization of that uniqueness.  It is what PHS dietary regimen is about.  And 
it's more than simply about food; it is about a way of life.  It is a way of life that 
evolves, a unique way of life; because eating so much demarks the way the life is 
structured.  And these two things, this deep respect for the intelligence of the vehicle 
and at the same time a respect that allows you to involve yourself in nurturing your 
vehicle precisely the way it's intended to be nurtured.  This is the best that you can 
do.

Type Allows Us to See How a Decision should be Made 
Think about it for a moment.  Type—what does type do? Type allows us to see how a 
decision should be made, to be very simple.  That's what type allows.  It allows us to 
see how a decision can be made and how a decision should be made; that is, should 
be made in the context of this is how you get to realize your uniqueness and all that 
stuff, and on and on and on.  But what we're looking at in the variable is that we're 
looking at something quite different.  We're looking at who, in a sense, makes the 
decision.  And when I say who, I mean it even more generically than that.  What I 
mean is the character.     
 
 
I love to call the maya, the movie.  And we are all characters in the movie.  And we 
have had a sense of that; that is, in the explorations of Profile and Incarnation Cross 
looking at roles.  We have a character in the movie and character comes with the 
thematics of characteristics.  If you can think about a playwright writing a play and 
they populate that play with certain characters, they provide a guideline to ultimately 
whoever will be the director.  They provide a guideline of that character's 
characteristics.   And that characteristic is not one side or the other; it’s not 
specifically just about their personality.  It can be about their body type, all kinds of 
things.  It is a kind of slot, a kind of generic description for a certain type of 
character.

time that I could truly establish a legitimate professional educational program, and 
that the graduates of this program carried in their education something unique and 
special and something of value that needed to be protected.

HD Books

is the real foundation for the future of this knowledge.  It is very exciting and it's all

Q: I just want to comment that this semester has completely filled me up.  I don't 
think I can think straight anymore.  It's going to take some integration to get what 
you've been saying somewhere where I can use it. 
 
Ra:  Yes, in many ways I feel the same way.  That is, I’m in an extraordinary place in 
my life.  I never thought I would be here.  I really didn't.  I'm about to close my third 
seven-year cycle.  I'm about to turn 60 years old, so I'm completing the Chinese 
round of five 12-year cycles.  I have completed all of my outlining of the mechanics 
of the knowledge that I was given.  I have figured out how everything works.  It's a 
very, very funny place to be.   
 
There are moments that I just sit very still and there is a kind of creeping emptiness 
around me.  I don’t know how to describe that.  It's very odd.  So I understand, 
because this first semester at the end of this year is the most profound experience I 
have ever had in my life.  I've never had anything like this. I have never operated at 
such a level of strategic consciousness, because that's what I am; I'm a pure 
strategic mind.  It has been really remarkable.  
 
I’ve watched the movie after all; I don't take credit for things. I know the joke.  I 
know that I'm just a passenger watching the movie.  And so I watch the movie, and 
I am often in such a state of wonder that I find it difficult sometimes just to stay with 
the program, to stay with the movie.  And the digestion of it is time.  We have time.  
We all have time.  Our value to the plane is one that grows with our growth.  We 
don't have to rush for it.  They will be there as we're ready.  This is the way that it 
is.   
 
We will all digested it in our own way.  It will take years, years and years and years 
for all of it to really sink in.  It's so much.  It’s not necessary for a human being to 
know this much, it really isn’t, I know that. I’m a practical man.  I know that you 
need to know what you need to know.  That's all.  But if you're going to teach, if 
you're going to be of service, then there is a burden to know more than you need to 
know—the nuance, the little things.  The glory of the 9th gate; God, I love that gate, 
what a wonderful thing, the miracle of one little thing after another.   
 
As an ego Manifestor I never understood such a thing, that if you slowly day-by-day, 
brick-by-brick do your work, you build the Great Wall of China.  It's amazing.  I've 
watched this happen, it is incredible, just little bits of fact, one after the other, year 
after year after year, and then you get to here.  You get to the glory that’s there in 
this image.  Wow, to be able to step out of the wheel, that's really something.

looking forward to it after all; otherwise, I wouldn’t do it.  It is something that I think 
is going to be fascinating, so let’s wait and see.
