Q:  That’s an interesting place.  I notice that when I do quote you to other people 
they immediately say, well, how do you know that.  I have a particular example.  I 
was just practicing explaining seven-centered and nine-centered, and they had the 
gate of doubt, they just said I don’t believe you, how do you know the seven-
centered and nine-centered.  It was so interesting to send me back to—I don’t know, 
find it integrated in me, so that I can express it. 
 
Ra:  Yes, it’s one of those questions that you can’t satisfy those people.  It’s like 
when people said to me: why 88°?  They were always asking me, why.  I can 
rationalize an answer for you, because you can see it in the mechanics.  If it were 
90° nothing would change, but that's not the answer.  The answer was: I don't 
know; that's what I was told.   
 
And of course, the same thing is true with the seven-centered to the nine-centered, I 
don't know.  The only possible proof that I could have and I’ve mentioned this many 
times before is that it's clear to me that the body temperatures of seven-centered 
and nine-centered beings were different; that is, seven-centered beings were cooler 
than nine-centered beings.  So, that would have been the only way of scientifically 
being able to say that there's something very different about the mechanisms.   
 
A lot of that has to do, and I know I get a lot of these questions over the years about 
the transition between seven-centered and nine-centered, but the development of 
the solar plexus system from being minor to major, from its seven-centered 
configuration to its nine-centered configuration is probably one of the main reasons 
of the heating up, but it's clear that the body temperature of nine-centered beings is 
much higher, we’re much more mutative.  It's a much higher body temperature than 
seven-centered beings. 
 
I never ask people to believe me.  The old-timers amongst you know that I would 
stand up in front of an audience in a public lecture the first thing I would say is: 
don't believe and don't trust me.  It's true.  If you’re going to believe me and trust 
me you’re crazy.  Don’t do that.  You have to trust your authority, after all.    
 
Human Design is provable in different ways.  Not in terms of how why there's this 
particular thing or that particular thing as an example, but the way in which it 
operates, the fact that it operates successfully, the way that these things that can be 
measured.  Type, for example, was substantiated just through simple statistical 
analysis, an analysis that is so clean that it is not something that could be made up, 
so, there are many, many different areas within the information that could be 
substantiated. 
 
For example, in the theme that, for example, Andrea is working on, being able to 
take people who know nothing about Design and you give one group their Strategy 
and Authority and their PHS, and you give another group just some kind of you know 
New Age fuzzy Bunny bullshit and then three weeks later you switch them around.  
The results are going to be as clear as clear can be.  We’re going to see that. 
 
The thing about Design is that it can be substantiated scientifically from many, 
many, many different levels.  But when it comes to why, when it comes to can you

prove going from seven- to nine-centered, no.  It would be nice, as I said, body

don't know.  Honestly I don't know. I don't know if the G stands for something, I 
don't.  My assumption it doesn't stand for anything.  It's actually the shape that

I don't know how to say Rave.  I just say it the way it's spelled.  But then again, I

Genoa: I guess you could say: if you dare.  It was interesting, after the radio show

inquired, and maybe that will development sometime. 
 
Ra: I think that more and more, obviously particularly through PHS and Rave 
Psychology—any psychologist who is really open will recognize that this is the only 
psychology that matters.  
 
[Written comment from Randy:  for me 7 center to 9 center was a process not an 
event ... in other words it took time before and after 1781] 
Ra: By the way, Randy, I agree with you. I agree with you in the sense that it 
certainly didn't happen on a dime.  I don't think, though, like the coming Raves I 
don't think that there were any seven-centered beings born after 1781. I doubt it.  
However, I think it was a gradual process over a 30 or 40 year period of the 
emergence of the nine-centered.  There may be a few anomalies, there always are, 
but certainly it was a gradual process.   
 
Again, like anything, it’s like people talking about their Kiron and the assumption 
that it happens on their Kiron return.  It starts 3½ years before, basically, and you 
get to feel it out about 3½ years later, and in all that stuff.  We’re always moving 
through processes.  I think that's true, particularly for 6th line beings to understand 
these transition points, that to go from the 3rd line phase to the roof phase isn't 
something that happens automatically.  As a matter of fact, some people never really 
get on the roof, because they never shake what happened to them in the Saturnian 
phase.  So, these are processes, there's no question about that.

Josette: It did put a big smile on my face, this segment.  It was a masterpiece. 
 
Ra:  Well, you know this is the wonder of luck.  I love the word ‘luck.’  I consider 
myself to be incredibly lucky.  And it is a lucky person that is ready to surrender to 
breakdown.  Not everybody is that lucky.  You have to be lucky; the gods have to 
smile on you.   
 
I have a number that runs around in my head and basically it's the 1% number.  You 
look at a hundred people who are in crisis and one of them is lucky.  And it is 
hopefully the possibility of our fractals, each and every one of us in our world that 
we live in, that that lucky person can find us or that we meet them, because then 
breakthrough is possible.  But until you embrace it—I mean, if you’re going to fall, 
fall, fall, fall; fall hard, and then you can get back up.  And so many people just can't 
do it, they just can't do it.  They can’t surrender to the breakdown.  You’ve got to be 
lucky.  You have to be lucky. 
 
I think I’m going to leave it on that note; that was a real good note.  I'm lucky to 
have all of you, I what you know that, and I mean that.  Well, enjoy your break; give 
you a chance to keep your brains from dissolving and give you a chance to dig in 
little.  If you want some real brain shattering show up in December for my heretical 
moment.  You shouldn't miss that one.  Anyway, to all of you, you take care, and do 
enjoy the movie, it’s a great one.  Until next time, whenever that is, bye for now.

The IHDS PHS Certification Year 3: PHS Practitioner  
 
The Primary Health System (PHS) is a revolutionary approach to well being.  PHS 
was first introduced by Ra Uru Hu in April 2003.  As empowering as Human Design is 
for the Personality Passenger, PHS is the awakening key for the Design Vehicle.  
 
PHS is the result of years of ground laying work in the sub-structure of the Line.  The 
PHS Practitioner Program Year Three focuses on the Practitioner Certification, Exami-
nations and Projects. 
 
The PHS Primary Health System Analyst program is open to any student who has 
completed PHS Analyst Year One program.  
 
The IHDS is the official Human Design and Global Incarnation Index certifying and 
licensing authority.  The IHDS offers programs leading to official certification in both 
teaching and practice.  
 
The PHS Primary Health System Practitioner is such a credit program.  To complete 
this program for certification, students are required to complete the entire program 
of three years.  
 
This book is a transcript of the third year, second semester originally taught in Win-
ter of 2008. 
 
 
 
~

Holistic Properties 1 
 
 
Hello and welcome back to all of you.  Before I actually get into today's work, I want 
to talk about my approach in terms of this semester particularly.  As you well know, 
this semester I will have a number of actual tests for you; really practice runs more 
than anything else.  But its time in which I begin to work differently with you.  For 
seven semesters I have been

it's time for a very different

similate all of this so that you can begin to work with all of this professionally.  So, 
my approach is going to be different and I will be asking you questions and I will be 
waiting for you to join in and respond. 
 
The Rave Properties 
The place where I want to begin is in trying to get you to a place where you start 
looking at a being from the database and interpreting the database without paying 
much attention—obviously, ultimately you‘re going to have to pay a lot of attention 
to it.  What I'm really getting at is that if you're looking at this particular illustration 
you really have two different stories, because the moment that I'm looking over here 
on this right is the moment that my attention is constantly going to be taken to the 
bodygraph.  We well know that the bodygraph reveals its story, its information.  All

The Variable 
But something is more intriguing for me.  And it begins with the very top of the list.  
That is, it begins with the Variable.  Now of course, the Variable is what we get to 
see in terms of the fixing of what is the Internal and the External both on the Perso-
nality and the Design side.  This is where the Variable comes from.  And in this par-
ticular case, you can see the Variable is Personality Left/Right, Design Right/Left.  
We have 16 different Variables.   
 
When Asi and I were working on the Design of MMAI, one of my priorities was to fi-
nally complete what I had envisioned as a property table, this rave properties.  And a 
property table that was going to be organized in—how shall I put this—the best way 
I can say this is that this ordering from Variable down to the not-self theme, because 
that's really what it is, the bottom part is just the data, but in going from the Varia-
ble down to the not-self theme is not a random ordering at all.  In other words, that 
placing Variable at the very top of this is something to recognize because in many 
ways it is representing everything, sort of.  Don‘t take that too literally.  Now, what 
is it?   
 
I start tomorrow morning 11 weeks of Variable analysis and it is one of those subject 
areas that intrigues me profoundly.  And it intrigues me profoundly for a very, very 
simple reason, or at least I try to keep it simple.  What creates these Variable confi-
gurations, the Left-ness and the Right-ness?  What creates this is the Tone.  And of 
course, what we know about Tone is that Tone is the architecture for our cognition.  
So one of the things to see very clearly is that if you take the four different kinds of 
cognition that are represented in what are those four different arrows, if you put this 
together, it is basically a self-reflective cognitive awareness structure.  In other 
words, it is a grouping of a specific way in which the potential for awareness oper-
ates.  It‘s all about cognition. 
 
And it's not about cognition from the point of view that it's a Personality concept.  It 
is not about the inner authority, per se.  It isn't, because it is something else.  When 
it comes together as a specific Variable it is a whole that is larger than the sum of its

The Rave Properties List

Where it gets different, where we enter into our specializations is when you get be-

the domain of either the Design or the Personality.  
 
Base Orientation 
Base orientation is something that is relatively new to you.  And Base orientation, as 
you'll begin to see, is something that is very fascinating and it is very fascinating be-
cause in fact, it gives us another one of these indicators of the way in which a vehicle 
or a Personality is going to operate.  When we‘re looking at the Design Base orienta-
tion and we see that it's a binary that this is a body that can go one way or another, 
and it really can.  It is a body that can, as an example, appear to be docile and sud-
denly be very active.  In other words, there's always a binary in the way in which 
this vehicle is going to work, sometimes tense and sometimes not.  There are always 
these kinds of things; there is no real one way for this body.   
 
Determination and Cognition 
Now, the determination we understand right away.  The determination is indirect, we 
have a nocturnal feeder.  We have somebody that is here to eat when the sun is 
down; this is the ideal for them.  And the physical cognition is one of touch.  Now, 
think about this for a moment.  Think about this body just at this level.  We have a 
body that we know is not going to be healthy if it's taking food into its system when 
there is a lot of ultraviolet light available.  We know that right away.   
 
But there's something else to see in that, indirect is indirect.  In other words, it is a 
body that doesn't necessarily come directly at you, as an example.  It's like looking 
at the cognition, this cognition of touch.  What you know is that this being needs to 
make physical contact; their body needs to make physical contact.  It doesn't mean 
their mind is going to be comfortable with it.  But their body needs that contact in 
order to get the information that it needs.  So they're going to give the appearance 
of being physically warm, as an example, physically putting out their body, the kind 
of person that touches you when they meet you, the kind of person that needs to 
have that kind of connection.  And it's not like it‘s something that they're thinking 
about.  It becomes part of the way in which they operate physically.   
 
Environment 
The environment—mountains—remember something about the environment.  The 
environment comes from the Design and it's coming from the Design External.  And 
the thing to recognize about the Externals is that we have different kinds of Va-
riables.  This is what's called the independent Variable.  Everything is based on this.  
The Internal is the dependent Variable.  It is dependent on the alignment of the ve-
hicle, the correctness of the vehicle in its movement in space.

One of the things so interesting about the environmental aspect is that the environ-
mental aspect is pointing you towards quality of life.  Quality of life is a combination 
of having the least resistance to deal with, whether that is resistance in terms of sit-
uations or resistance in terms of the other.  In essence, it is the protected environ-
ment for the particular being, in this case mountains.   
 
Again, if you go back and you review the material on mountains you'll see that we're 
really talking about the high ground.  This is somebody that always likes to take the 
high ground.  And of course, in English that's a double entendre, there are many 
meanings that are there.  The fact is this person in essence likes to look down on 
things in order to figure them out.  It likes to get above things, which means it 
doesn't necessarily like to mix things up. 
 
Rave Properties Show Possibilities in the Being 
One of the things that is so important is to begin to allow your creative process to 
latch onto these keynotes and begin to see how they become a possibility in a be-
ing‘s life.  Remember something about the properties.  The reality is that this is 
about being a PHS practitioner for beings who've already entered into the experi-
ment of their Human Design, because there is no point otherwise, because most of 
the information that is here in the rave properties is going to be lost in the dysfunc-
tion of not-self.  There's no point talking about Base orientation and determination 
cognition and environment when you're dealing with somebody that cannot operate 
correctly.  It doesn‘t work.  
 
What we‘re looking at here is a possibility.  You're looking at the potential that needs 
to be filled in, that possibility that looms there.  It's always the same thing for me, 
for years I've described the nature of Profile.  Profile is a costume and you can see 
that in Profiles that operate without cognition, you can recognize the Profile in them.  
But of course there is all that dysfunction that is there.  They‘re not really wearing it 
well. And they end up with all of the negative aspects that are inherent in the Profile.  
It is a spectrum like anything else.  And those aspects end up being very, very diffi-
cult to deal with.  Three-fives who are not-self have a really hard time.  And yet, at 
the same time, this is an extraordinary potential to be able to discover, to be able to 
place things out into the world, to really be of value in that sense. 
 
But again, in looking at the properties, the properties are what we are honing some-
body towards.  Let's take a step back.  Let's think about somebody that comes to 
you.  They‘re into their design, they‘re fascinated by what they've heard about PHS, 
and they come to you.  One of the things to realize in looking at them and under-
standing that in order for them to be able to truly learn from this process that what 
we have in front of them in terms of the properties, at least what we get to see in 
terms of the properties is a kind of measuring stick.  It is a kind of signpost of how 
close they are to the full potential of the inherent properties that are there in their 
design.  These are their inherent properties.  You can call it whatever, inherent 
traits, gifts, possibilities, potentials.

No Limitation 
And more than that, there‘s something very, very special to understand, we have no 
idea where this leads to.  I no longer accept that there is such a thing as limitation.  
I don‘t.  It's clear to me that within uniqueness and in the ability to operate uniquely 
that there are no rules.  There aren‘t, there can't be, which means that concepts 
about what is possible or not possible don't mean anything.  All we‘re doing as a 
practitioner is we‘re trying to guide our clients, we‘re trying to guide them towards 
the fulfillment of these properties.  And they will fulfill the properties through the 
unique configuration that is there.  But they still need to fulfill each of those, in that 
sense.   
 
So, what we're doing is that we begin a process of guiding them.  It's like looking at 
the Base orientation, the binary, and seeing that they‘re indirect.  You know the way 
their body is going to function in the daylight and the way their body is going to 
function when the sun goes down is going to be totally different; very different kind 
of body, very different way in which the body is going to react.  One of the things 
that we are going to be able to see is that if they enter into the experiment of their 
PHS regimen and they begin the process of following the indirect determination, one 
of the things that we're going to see out of them, and one of the things that we can 
project for them is that they're going to develop very different kinds of patterns for 
the daytime activities and their night time activities, or the sunlight activities and the 
lack of sunlight activity.

PHS is Dangerous 
PHS is something that's very dangerous.  And I mean that it's very dangerous within.  
We have a certain protection, all of us.  We are abnormal, and we are involved in 
breaking the covenant with the program.  And yet at the same time enough of our 
behavior passes for normal so that we‘re not ostracized from our societies.  One of 
the things that PHS does, though, is that it puts all that at risk.  It really does.  And 
it puts it at risk because it sets up a momentum for establishing fully the unique au-
thority that can defend itself from homogenization.   
 
It doesn't matter whether or not you are operating correctly according to your Strat-
egy and Authority.  As long as your vehicle isn‘t operating according to its PHS you 
cannot avoid the homogenization that takes place within the vehicle.  It's like what I 
was mentioning to you earlier, Stephen, about your cold.  It‘s the same kind of thing.  
You deal with a homogenized environment and it's very, very difficult to avoid con-
tamination from that environment.  But if you're in the right environment, you do 
avoid it.  It‘s one of the most extraordinary things about the Design External is that 
this is the protective bubble, this is the environment that is the nurturer of the secu-
rity of one's bonds and one's lifestyle, one's longevity, one's well-being, one's spirit. 
 
It‘s not something that you can decide.  None of this is about decision making, but it 
really is about understanding that for this particular being the high ground is so im-
portant, the high ground where they can have the physical contact, the high ground 
where they can have the physical contact and live out their night life.  This is them 
and this is what their vehicle is going to demand if it's correct.  It doesn't mean that 
the Personality gets it, by the way, it doesn‘t.

Personality and Design 
Personality and Design, they are very far away from each other and they‘re very dif-
ferent from each other.  They really are.  And of course the Personality is always at a 
disadvantage because it is not running the show.  That is, it's not the vehicle.  And 
though the not-self can certainly get in the way of that and distort all that, the mo-
ment the not-self is present you kiss these properties goodbye and you go back to 
go, don't collect $200, no get-out-of-jail-free card, you go back to go and you start 
all over again.   
 
PHS changes fundamentals in the way in which you're going to live.  It just does.  
And it creates enormous dilemmas.  I‘m deeply aware of the fact that PHS at this 
stage can be of deep value for personal development; yet at the same time, create a 
lot of dilemmas.  I see it myself.  I happen to be very fortunate that I get to live in 
my environment.  But my children who have different environments, my partner, we 
all have four different food regimens; there are all kinds of complexities that arise 
out of this.  
 
2027 
I think with the knowledge of PHS what I'm reminded of is the stress that the Voice 
placed on 2027, this change that is coming.  I've said this before but this is why the 
knowledge was given as a preparation for the changing of this cycle, to be able to 
prepare human beings to be able to operate out of their own authority and to oper-
ate out of their own authority in an age of diminished fertility, a diminished fertility 
that's going to mean that lives are going to be lived out and the maturation of those 
lives are going to be lived out very differently vis-à-vis relationships with the other, 
that the sexual bond, because of the impact on the 55 and the 59, is no longer going 
to be a dominant thing for the survival of the species.   
 
As a matter of fact, it is something that again is going to lack the attention of the 
program and is something that is going to begin to fade.  So much of what PHS is 
about is about the power and freedom that exists in an individual as opposed to 
somebody who is bonded.  And we all know that, we all know that.  The genetic im-
perative has trapped us, basically, and has trapped us in the bond and we all know 
how good we feel in those occasions when we can step outside of the bond and not 
feel that we have to be responsible or that we have to pay attention to the needs or 
demands or concerns of others relative to what it is that we are doing.   
 
This is something for another age, it really is.  It is not even necessarily for our child-
ren, because I think that it will create as I said social dilemmas unless they bond to-
gether in the sense that—I see, obviously, there is some level of movement out 
there for communities of people involved in Human Design.  I have always assumed 
that at some point or another, these things would exist.  It's clear that in those kinds 
of environments that certainly it would make an enormous difference in terms of be-
ing able to share the difference and the pleasure of the differentiation.  However, in 
the general sense it‘s the grandchildren and the great-grandchildren from now.

They're the ones who are going to have not just the opportunity; they‘re really going

We forget that we lived forever as seven-centered beings in a Saturnian cycle.  We

generations since the transition of 1781 that begins to grasp the fact that it takes a 
long time now for us to be mature.  And when you have the diminished impact of the 
sexual imperative and you have an extended maturation process that the potential of 
PHS for the well-being of somebody is extraordinary.  I mean, it really is.   
 
And again, there you have a situation where here is somebody where the environ-
ment is the mountains and they don't have to deal with the situation in which yes 
this would be wonderful for me, but, I can't.  I can't because I have a partner, I have 
children, I have this, and I have that, whatever the case may be.  Again, it is really 
something to grasp.  And it doesn't matter whether your partner‘s aware and into 
Human Design, and so on and so forth.  It really doesn't yet, because it is so deeply, 
deeply personal.  And the fact that you have a bond with somebody else doesn't 
mean that you actually have a natural tendency to be in the same environment at 
the same time.  It doesn‘t.  
 
And again, I want you to be very careful and not to misunderstand this and begin to 
make judgments about your relationships in this way.  It is our job to be the seed of 
this knowledge.  You are the first generation.  We carry the seed of the knowledge, 
and we can take certain benefits from it.  There's no question that following your de-
termination is something that can bring enormous benefits to the way in which you 
operate as a physical being.  There's no question about it.   
 
I've seen the result over and over and over and over again.  It is simply something 
that obviously is something that the body relishes to be able to function out of its 
determination, and that that determination in and of itself is something that can be 
of great value to somebody who is entering into their process, particularly those who 
have been deeply conditioned; Projectors, as an example, where it can be of great 
value for them to really begin the deeper cleansing process, the deeper de-
homogenization process by taking advantage of PHS.   
 
The Single Authority 
But again, it is something that we have to deal with and understand that the deeper 
we go into these properties, the deeper we go into the domain of the single authority 
that cannot afford dependency, the single authority that lives by what is correct for it 
regardless of the circumstances, regardless of the consequences.  And that is not 
something that we understand yet.  It seems enormously selfish.   
 
It's always amusing to me, you look at the transition of the global cycle when it 
moves into the 55th Gate, the first thing that it does is that it hits the 6th Line, the 
55.6, selfishness.  We‘re going to have decades and decades of selfishness as this 
theme.  And to see what this all means, to understand our work in preparing the

I don't see it as a negative, please understand that.  I don't think that everything is

because of course, this is what it's all about, to begin to see what it is to be a Gene-

3/5, to live out the essence of being a triple-split, to live out the deep power of emo-
tional awareness, to live out your cross.  This is what it's all about.  Each and every 
one of us, as you move down the properties one by one by one, this is the setup.  
And once you can recognize them, you're recognizing them out of the uniqueness 
that is you, your unique take on this construct, this framework. 
 
Dedication to the Process 
At the beginning of the process, everything is really about this.  And it begins with a 
caution.  It begins with caution to anyone who is going to enter into the Center for 
Human Differentiation, anybody who enters into that process, to understand that this 
is what we are after and whether or not they are ready to make the sacrifice that is 
necessary.  It is one thing to experiment with your strategy and inner authority, but 
it is another thing entirely to enter into the process of enhancing, cleansing, and 
bringing about what is a sensitivity to your form that in essence has never existed 
before, because it calls for a dedication that is very special.  Not everybody is going 
to be capable of that.   
 
The fact is that determination is something that is most easily established in babies.  
The ideal of positive conditioning, if one can put it that way, to start them off that 
way so that it will, because it is correct for them, always be the only way after that, 
because anything else will make them feel uncomfortable.  Again, it is a dilemma for 
us when we‘re dealing with somebody who has spent most of their life as not-self.  
There is so much work to be done.  And not everybody is ready to operate at this 
level.  Not everybody has the dedication for that.  And you cannot judge it by your-
self or this classroom, after all.

who show up every day, and there are so many of them now.  At their level of Hu-

Healing Starts With the Body 
The only way that you can heal anybody is that you start with their body.  This is the 
great gift that we bring.  And we're not going to be able to avoid having to deal with 
all of those beings who think that PHS is a panacea or who think PHS is a diet or

that you do that is not correct for you, the result is homogenization.

other area.  This is a place where you have to deal with an absolute, because that's 
what it is.  You want to enhance the vehicle?  Here‘s the absolute.   
 
Limitations in Following PHS 
I still don't follow my PHS one hundred percent.  I don't.  As a Manifestor father to 
begin with if I don't have a meal with my family, I barely ever have anything to do 
with them, in a sense.  It is very, very difficult.  I just try to deal with it as best that 
I can and it seems to be sort of okay.  I take two meals a day by myself.  My deter-
mination is calm.  And most of the meals are in fact rather calm.  But of course, 
when they are, that's a disadvantage for my oldest son who is the opposite of me 
who's nervous and needs the stimulation.  There are all of these things that come 
into all of this. 
 
It‘s why I so clearly understand the limitation of it in the sense of what happens to 
somebody in any kind of family situation, bonded situation, the pressure that is 
there.  As I said, this knowledge is really for what is coming.  Yet at the same time, 
it's not like we put it aside and say, we‘re going to build up all of this theoretical in-
formation until we get 40 or 50 years from now when it‘s really going to be useful.  
That‘s not the point.  We can all take advantage of it.

no question about that.

authenticity of their process and you cannot do that with deep homogenization.  In 
other words, PHS for somebody who is Right fixed mentally is something that is 
going to be an important tool in helping them begin to get to the hidden qualities 
that are there in their cognitive potential.

Food and Homogenization 
In other words, what we're dealing with here is we're dealing with aligning the ve-
hicle to its precise needs.  And these precise needs are, in fact, differentiated needs.  
The real differentiation doesn't really emerge until you see the being holistically, be-

core in the center here, this is what fundamental to the work that we have been 
doing, and understanding that if we are truly going to transform the vehicle, then it 
has to do primarily with food.  This is something very basic to grasp.   
 
It is the greatest agent of conditioning in the life from the moment you emerge into 
this life, from the moment that bottle, that spoon is being stuck into your mouth, the 
moment you come into this life you are homogenized and you are taken away from 
what is healthy for you specifically.  And you were opened up to all the homogenized 
problems as a result.  Most of the dilemmas, most of the difficulties, most of the 
physical ailments, are a byproduct of vehicles that are not allowed to be natural, but 
vehicles that are conditioned to operate in a different way, to be less responsive, to 
carry more weight, to be this, to be that, whatever the case may be.   
 
We also know that there is a psychological bridge that needs to be crossed in order 
to get to a place in which such a recommendation, the PHS regimen, is something 
that can be realistically embraced.  After all, PHS is actually knowledge that was de-
signed for babies coming into the world.  That's what it's for, that's when it's natural 
and correct and easy, all the way down the line, because there is no conditioning, 
there is only the natural nurturing of the young. 
 
We‘re not like, we know that.  And of course the dilemma of our lifetime is all of this 
incorrect nutrition that we've put into our vehicle.  Again, it doesn't mean that the 
food was ―bad‖ for us; it has nothing to do with that.  It was going to maintain us in 
the homogenized pattern of what it was to be a physical human being.  But it cer-
tainly wasn't going to allow us to be able to develop what would be our differentiated 
physicality.

So we need to be able to remind them.  One of the things that's very important is

and they are interested in having an appointment with a PHS practitioner, that that 
very first meeting between the practitioner and the client is basically the practitioner 
going over their design, because the practitioner knows what the key is going to be.  
And at the end of that process with them to set it up in such a way as you ask them, 
you invite them, you inform them, or whatever the case may be, to make the deci-
sion of whether they want to really enter into this process.  If they think, if they feel 
they are ready for that.   
 
It's not simply a matter of they come, you give them a little bit of information, you 
send them along their way and you‘re available for feedback, because we're differ-
ent.  And we all well know that the battle begins with that first couple of years of 
dealing with the dilemma of your mind and the possibility of what being correct can 
be for you.  I don't want anybody to be able to walk into that center and simply buy 
a piece of information that they think that they can use, because I'm very concerned 
about the way in which PHS is brought out to the public.  It is something that is im-
portant for me. 
 
Providing a Holistic Service 
And it also is very important for me that the role of the practitioner is the equivalent 
of Human Design‘s family doctor, the kind of doctor in the 50s that if you were sick 
would come over to your house and look after you, if you know what I mean.  In 
other words, that this is more than simply a place where you get a reading, this is 
where a unique kind of relationship is established, and a relationship that is there to 
go quite deep, as a matter of fact, and to be able to provide that client with some-
body that they can truly trust and rely on as they go through that process. 
 
It‘s also very important for us as scientists.  Again, we‘re dealing with a new science.  
Our observations, our ability to take information from our clients, to gather this in-
formation is something that is very, very important.  Again, I want you to grasp how 
different a role this is.  This is not simply about giving out a prescription, it really 
isn't.  It is about providing a holistic service.  It‘s a very special service.  For me 
there is no example on this plane of anything that is there to provide for us, that is, 
to provide for nine-centered beings that are aware of their mechanics.  There is 
nothing; there is only us. 
 
And for me the development of you as professionals, both practitioners and psychol-
ogists, is the first generation of beings that are trained to be of service to nine-
centered beings.  For me this is an enormous step in our ability to be of service to 
whoever in humanity is ready for this kind of process.  And it means that the roles 
that we play out within this context as practitioner/psychologist are that we are truly 
there to be a very deep intimate guide for the other.  And it all begins with our ability

Recognition and Healing 
This is one of the great gifts that Projectors bring into the world.  I think one of the 
most profound tools for healing is recognition.  In the factory hospitals of the world, 
where the doctor barely has time to ever know who you are and in many cases will 
never meet you, never see you eye-to-eye, that your inner trust in what is going to 
be the result of all that is deeply, deeply diminished.   
 
This is something so important to understand about knowing the other.  You're not 
just putting them at ease.  You are setting up the ability to truly be able to commu-

work and study, that outer authority that is simply there for them and they're ready 
to take it because you know how to deal with them, you know how to approach 
them.  This is what gives us such a tremendous advantage.  And so often it's just 
ignored.   
 
One of the things that will be there in the Center, and by the way it will open in Sep-

there, so you know how to deal with them.  It makes an enormous difference.

And it's going to be very, very important because it's obvious, the complexity that is

stomach.

about the dilemmas of their emotional system, about what it means for them to wait,

The Determination and the Cognition 
So they have to be ready, and when they're ready then that process can begin. And 
think about what that process brings.  Let‘s just talk about the combination here of

to change.  Anybody who enters into their PHS notices immediately that obviously it 
is going to impact the way in which you operate in your life.  Food is so deeply con-

It is going to make a difference whether this person is single, whether this person is 
with one person, a partner or lover, or whether this person has a family, because as 
we well know as I've explained to you in my own context that to have a family leads 
to very, very complex issues when it comes to eating.  And for many modern fami-
lies, and particularly families in the West, it‘s the only time they‘re normally togeth-

So, all of these things have to be taken into consideration.  Again, it‘s part of the re-

in that initial meeting you need to have a conversational level with them as well.  
You need to find out certain things about their life, again to find out whether they‘re 
single or with somebody, so forth and so on.  Find out where they work, their envi-
ronment, all the kinds of things that ultimately are going to impact them so you have 
a real sense of who you are dealing with.  The deeper you know them, the more suc-
cessful your relationship is going to be.  And what I mean by that is the success of 
the relationship is the transformation that can take place in the client.

extraordinary colic, they just will.  They‘re going to have all kinds of problems with 
gas and all kinds of things because of the homogenization taking place in the day 
feeding.  In order for this person to enter into that indirect you cannot just say to

Sharing Information 
I don't have all the data, you know that.  I have the mechanics, I have the frame-

only the basic mechanical rules; nothing more, nothing less.  And every time I'm 
asked a question, and you all know that because you've heard them, every time I'm 
asked that question my answer is, yes, that‘s possible, because it is a nuance within 
the general theme.   
 
And what is always clear to me is that we are differentiated beings and what cave-
man means for one person is different than what caveman will mean for somebody 
else and yet they'll both be following the generic of that regimen, but it will be effec-
tive for each of them in their different way.  So there are nuances in this.

their process relative to being similar.  In other words, ones that might have the 
same determination and ultimately those for example that have the same environ-
ment, to begin to see how they are living out the possibility of that environment and 
how significant that is for them.

have those that are participating in every kind of environment.  It's part of the whole

Lesson Three 
Review and Test Preparation 
 
 
Welcome to all of you and to the few of you that are downloading.  This is really a 
historic moment for Human Design.  It is one that is very important to me and it's 
very important to the future and integrity of these programs, that is, PHS and Rave 
Psychology.  They obviously are, as you're familiar with by now, the fact that these 
are the only programs in Human Design that are going to be tested.  That it is an 
essential ingredient in order to establish the professionalism involved. 
 
I‘ve had a vision for a very long time.  That is, I guess it began about 10 years ago 
when I first started to think about how to develop the physical and psychological as-
pects of the knowledge.  It was clear to me that the nine-centered being was some-
thing different.  And that the procedures, the human services as we had known 
them, as had developed out of the seven-centered history that they were no longer 
things that truly served us.  It is clear that even a basic understanding of Human De-
sign offers more in terms of stabilizing the Personality than any kind of traditional 
psychology has ever been able to achieve.  It is simply mechanical and directly to 
the point.  
 
The Center for Human Differentiation 
It is clear and will become clearer over time that the PHS regimen is something that 
has enormous capacity, enormous potential to provide secure well-being physically in 
a human life.  That vision was an environment in which nine-centered beings could 
be served.  It is what the Center for Human Differentiation is all about and it is truly 
my great good fortune that I have not only the vision and the knowledge but the 
allies to be able to realize something that is so far ahead of its time, in a sense.  It 
has made my work much, much, much easier to have had Asi Farran as are our 
technology whiz, because that is literally what he is.   
 
The Center for Human Differentiation is going to be truly a state-of-the-art environ-
ment, and frankly unlike anything you can even imagine, even from the little bit I 
have told you.  We've been doing developmental work on the environment itself.  
And it is really going to be quite a place.  I'll tell you more about it as it is pertinent 
to share it with you.  But it will be up and running in September and it will really be 
something.  And it is not able to fulfill its potential without the staff that is going to 
be there, that is going to bring this knowledge and be able to provide with this know-
ledge, services that really are going to be of enormous value to those who have en-
tered into their experiment.

Education in Human Design 
It is clear to me, because we are going to be dealing with the physical well-being of 
human beings and their psychological well-being and because we are going to be of-
fering this directly as a service to them that it is essential for me that only the high-
est standard of staff is acceptable, nothing less than the highest standard.  For me 
this educational program, and quite a few of you take both programs, which is quite 
a thing to do, these are the most complex and most sophisticated teachings ever in 
Human Design, ever organized into single programs.  Nothing in Human Design has 
ever been given so much dedicated time for exploration and expansion and the de-
velopment of analytical skills and a deep, deep knowledge base.   
 
For me, anyone who is going to be a PHS Practitioner or a Rave Psychologist stands 
head and shoulders above anyone in Human Design in terms of being able to use the 
knowledge as deeply as possible for the benefit of the other.  And in that way they 
become the standards for me of what it means to master this knowledge.  More than 
that, my security in that those that are going to come to the center, those that are 
going to be involved in long-term therapeutic processes, what I would prefer to think 
of as outer authority environments, people who are going to spend their time in that 
that they are guaranteed the best quality that we can offer in terms of the know-
ledge and in terms of the dedication to the work.  And at the same time, that means 
that the client is protected and we are only going to get the best possible results out 
of that.   
 
All of this is one of the keys for me.  When I first built the basic Jovian Archive web-
site, when it first started, already in the plans was this kind of environment.  It never 
got realized because there was just no point in being able to do it until there was a 
group of beings properly educated.  The other thing is something very important for 
you to understand about this whole testing system.  Aside from making sure that the 
best level is represented, I also want you to understand that it brings you extraordi-
nary protection, that is, the protection from unlawful and illegal competition.  In oth-
er words, people who claim to be a practitioner or a psychologist, people who claim 
to have this knowledge who do not.   
 
The fact that this is a tested program, aside from the amount of time involved, that 
it is tested and certified means that we have the legal framework to deal with any-
one who tries to pretend that they are these things.  And by the way, we are actively 
involved.  This year we will take our first legal actions in protection of the rights of 
Human Design.  I want to make certain that a little bit of knowledge which people 
can grab here and there doesn't become the framework of misleading people and 
creating problems for them.  
 
So, the testing, in that sense, has importance at many different levels, and it's 
something that I just wanted to be able to share with you and also share with you 
the historic nature of it, because it really is.  It is for me the maturing of this whole 
educational process and that these programs are as sophisticated as any program

Examination Schedule 
Okay, let's get to some of the basics here.  This is the basic outline for candidate 
programs; that is, PHS and Rave Psychology.  You can see that first of all, the com-
ponents of it are broken up into two parts and the first part is made up of online ex-
aminations.  The first one as you've already seen is for next week.  There will be 
another one in the 10th week, that is, the week of March 10th, so it will be on the 11th 
of March.  That will be the second one in this semester.  There will be two in the next 
semester.  So, altogether there are four online examinations. 
 
 
 
You can see that each of the online examinations is worth 15 percentage points of 
your grading overall.  And that they are progressively more difficult.  In other words, 
it is very conscious on my part, that is, starting off with what is general knowledge 
and slowly getting more sophisticated in terms of the questioning.  So as you can 
see there are the four examinations, each of them that is worth 15% of the grade, 
and of course the thesis project, which is the other 40%.

Just to go over this and then I'll come back and look at various aspects with you.  
The whole grading system—if you're below 50% for the year, that is, all of this, then 
you're required to audit the third year again.  In other words, go through the pro-
gram again before you would be allowed to do retesting.  If you're between 50% and 
74%, you don't have to audit the next year.  In other words, it is not a requirement.  
That is, you can register for retesting and there will be a special testing regimen that 
will be available probably in October of this year for those who wanted to be re-
tested.  Anyone who gets over 75% or higher, this is certification.  So as you can see 
the standard bar is placed relatively high.  And I think that's very important.   
 
Relax and Enjoy the Testing 
I think it‘s also important for you not to get caught up in, because I‘ll talk about ap-
proaches in a moment, how to approach this, I simply want you to go through this 
relaxed and enjoy it as much as you can.  The fact is that you've all taken in a tre-
mendous amount of knowledge.  I am very confident in all of you, but everybody has 
different reactions under testing regimens.  Not everybody works well under pres-
sure, all of these different things.   
 
So I want you to understand that nobody who goes through this process is denied 
the potential for the certification.  In other words, it's not that you fail and you're out 
and about all that stuff.  It also means that you can put off testing.  That is your 
right.  Nobody is being pressured into all of this, you have to do what you feel is cor-
rect for you.  I suggest, by the way, that if you are thinking of such a thing to take 
the first exam anyway.  I think you need to find your confidence in this kind of a 
process and be comfortable within yourself and in that way you can make a much 
clearer decision about the way in which you want to approach this. 
 
But it's very important for me because the people who are going to be coming in the 
door of the Center are people who have begun their experiment with Human Design 
and they need the best possible guidance.  They‘ve made a huge step in their life 
and they need the best possible support.  I think it's very important that that is there 
for them.   
 
And so, this testing regimen yes, the standard is high.  I wrote the first exam today, 
that is, what you'll take next week as your PHS exam.  When I first started I actually 
tested people.  There are a couple of people that took tests.  Martin you certainly 
took them, Andrea I think you took them.  I don‘t know anyone else in that genera-
tion, Alok, for sure.  He took one of the weirdest ones, one of the first ones that was 
impossible to do.  It was all trick stuff.  But there were tests; there were really tests 
in the beginning. 
 
It's just interesting after all of these years I really enjoyed writing it, I must say.  I 
don't know how much I'll enjoy correcting them, but nonetheless.  I certainly en-
joyed writing it.  It was a very, very interesting experience to look within myself, to 
see what comes out in terms of what I think is a good question and that's funny for 
me, because that's not my trip.  I‘m not a 63 after all, I‘m a 4.  I‘m wise about ques-

tions.  The danger for me is always getting too clever, so it was very interesting to

The Technical Aspect of the Tests 
First of all, about the technical aspect of this, because the technical aspect is really 
brilliant, if you could see what I see, because I see behind a veil in the backdoors 
that Asi has created, these administration places, the ability to be able to create on-
line examinations—by the way, I could go between straight questions, which is what 
this first exam is, I can also choose to set up multiple choices.  And ultimately by the 
way, as teachers, if you want to develop testing for students, not necessarily as a 
grade, but for them to really get a sense of how well they're grasping the knowledge, 
ultimately, I will make this facility available because it's brilliant.   
 
So, for example, I'm able to put up, post a question with its own illustration, if I 
want.  Or in the case of the examination that you have next week there are ten gen-
eral questions and then there are five questions that are related to a single chart.  
So I can put up a single chart as a standard, and all these kinds of things.  It‘s fasci-
nating the way in which it works.  And then post it and give it a date and time.   
 
You'll notice that it has a specific time.  That is, next week the exam window opens 
at what is 17:15 GMT.  So 15 minutes after what our normal class time is.  And by 
the way, I will be in the class at the normal time, that is, 20 minutes before its nor-
mal opening.  So, if you have last-minute jitters or you just want to have company 
or you have questions or whatever, you can.  The actual examination will be in 15 
minutes afterwards.  You'll have to go out of the main class.  And what you've no-
ticed today when you came in you saw that underneath where we normally go into 
the class there's the examination box and in that examination box there is a button 
that will appear that says take exam and that will open up at exactly 15 minutes af-
ter the hour.   
 
You get 45 minutes for the exam: 15 questions, 45 minutes.  If you go over the 
time, there‘s nothing that stops it after 45 minutes, but you'll be penalized for going 
beyond the time because you‘re not supposed to.  And then what happens is that it 
gets posted to a file for me where I'm able to grade it.  The moment that the grading 
of it is done and I finish the grading, you automatically get an e-mail with your grade 
and a copy of the examination and how you answered it.  So it's an incredibly effi-
cient system.  I'm very impressed with it and I think that it's one of those things 
about having good tools.  It's a really good tool. 
 
Right Mind Studying 
Before I open this up, because I assume that you have questions, there is something 
that I want to talk about that I think is very, very important and it has to do with 
whether you are Left or Right minded.  That is, if you are Left on the Personality side 

--- стр 46 ---
PHS YEAR 3, SEMESTER 2: PHS Practitioner Certification  
A Digital Book for Students 
PHS is a certificate program of the (IHDS) International Human Design School 
All Rights Reserved.  Copyright 2006 Jovian Archive Corporation 
38 
or right on the Personality side.  In terms of preparation for any examination or any 
testing it is essential to grasp this, because otherwise you just do yourself a lot of 
damage.   
 
First things first, dealing with the right, obviously the right is receptive, it is its na-
ture, it takes things in.  It is an absorber.  If you are right minded do not put your-
self under the pressure of studying.  In other words, don't put yourself under the 
pressure of trying to figure out what you think you think I think, what you think I 
think I will put there.  In other words, don't get into that whole process of trying to 
figure out that I have to cover this and I should cover that and I‘m weak over here 
so I‘m going to cover this over there.  This is very, very unhealthy if you're right.  
It‘s not the way your mind works and it is not what is healthy for you. 
 
What is healthy is, look through material because you enjoy looking through the ma-
terial.  And don't worry about whether when you're looking at this material you 
should be looking at something else.  Just take things in.  And don't take things in 
for a specific reason that you think that this is what the subject is going to be.  In 
other words, just relax.  It's already in you.  If you are right, it is already in you.  It's 
there.  And one of the most important things to do in this life is to trust your gift.  
It‘s very, very difficult for the right, living in a strategically oriented plane where the 
not-self is still living out the old strategic way of the seven-centered being, where 
everything is about strategy.  Well, it's not for the right.   
 
So, when you're preparing for an examination you're not preparing at all.  You're 
not; you're just going about your business, going about your business enjoying, be-
cause you do, I know that, enjoying your process of going through this material and 
working with it.  It‘s more important for somebody who's Right, simply to be busy 
working with charts and investigating them for clients or otherwise than to be con-
cerned about just digging in and trying to figure out this or that.   
 
So that is my advice to the Right, and it's good, good advice.  So, take it in.  Save 
yourself the grief, because the moment that you try to focus on the strategy, the 
moment you think, oh my god, I don't remember anything about that, you‘re 
screwed, you really are.  You‘re going to be so confused then when the questions 
come up, because you have this thing that you were trying to stick in front of you 
that isn't supposed to be there.  So let go of all that. 
 
Left Mind Studying 
Now, if you‘re Left, this is a different story.  Everything is strategy, and you haven't 
taken it all in, in the same way.  Strategic beings take things in according to what 
they're interested in, to what is part of the world around them, to what their basic 
focus is.  They often don't pay attention to those things that don't have anything to 
do with what seems to concern them.  And there are often areas where they‘re very 
weak.   
 
 

--- стр 47 ---
LESSON THREE 
Review & Test Preparation 
PHS is a certificate program of the (IHDS) International Human Design School 
All Rights Reserved.  Copyright 2006 Jovian Archive Corporation 
39 
 
So, if you're strategic you've got to look at your knowledge base and say, where‘s 
my weakest area and go there and dig into it.  This is what it is to be strategic.  
Strategic is that you have to weigh things.  Okay, I know this pretty good.  And I 
sort of know that okay, but oh that over there, I don't remember a thing.  This is 
strategic thinking.  
 
And if you're Right and your mind is working like that, you‘re not doing a very good 
job with your not-self.  You have to deal with it.  This is the great challenge for the 
Right, just breathe, and work with the knowledge.  But if you're Left, you can't afford 
to be blind or arrogant about where you stand, nor can you be the other side of that.  
Oh, shit, I don't know anything, because it‘s not true.  To be strategic is to be able to 
see very clearly where you don't need to put energy but where you do need to put 
the energy, where you need to put the focus.  So it's two very, very distinct 
processes.

Written question: the questions and answers will be written or spoken?

do not reward essays.  I really appreciate brevity.  It‘s very important in answering 
these questions, because the moment that the answer is too long I'm always suspi-
cious that the person really doesn't get it.  So brevity is something that I really ap-
preciate.  And these questions are designed to be answered directly, in that sense.

just looking for the answer.

and the ability to communicate however one is communicating, whether it‘s audio, 
audiovisual, whether it is a PowerPoint and written, and so forth and so on.  Whatev-
er medium is being used you have to see that you have to be able to provide some-
thing that can measure up to 40% of what your grade is.  It has to be something

sary.  And it's necessary as early as possible, particularly when you're dealing with

dealing with dependent Right Variables PHS is an essential key.  It gives them a 
head start in being able to come to grips with the dilemma of what it is for them to 
be Right.

different world in which Design and Personality are really very different from each 
other.

ly.  As best they could I would recommend that they break down the meals that they 
currently eat into their individual components and then see what the components are 
made out of, for instance the sandwich that has the components and the bread be-
comes the complex, separate ingredient that you can say that the bread has more 
than one ingredient so to slowly dismantle their traditional eating and to begin 
through touch, through what feels right for them, to in a very sensory level explore 
the right foods and of course respond to the correct foods as the Generator to see if 
they can feel their response mechanism aligning with what the recommendation of-
fers, so that they can begin to recognize for themselves that they are getting the 
right practical food that feels right for the system.   
 
And over time their body, with my own experience, their body will make the deter-
mination that they will not be able to consume the multiple ingredient food without a 
cost extracted in terms of its goal or even mental confusion and that they can begin

6th Tone: Touch, Acceptance and the Rave 
Question:  Ra, someone asked, why acceptance for the 6th touch.  I can understand

Ra: It has to do with what it is to be a rave.  A rave is part of a conscious penta, and 
as a part of a conscious penta it gives up its identity, something that we will never 
do, but something that it is forced to do.  The 6th Tone is actually the Tone that binds 
the conscious penta together.  That is, it is bound together through the frequency 
phenomenon of the cognitive touch.  It is acceptance of a greater life.  That is, it is 
acceptance of being an aspect or a component of a larger whole, and it is the 6th 
Tone that makes the bridge as all 6s do, that makes the bridge to the surrender of 
individual identity and the acceptance of a larger consciousness.   
 
In that sense, it is very profound.  That is, it is what we are unable to participate in.  
That is, we have enough trouble bonding with somebody, let alone to give up our 
identity to be nothing more than a component in a larger life.  That‘s what the rave 
is designed for.  And the 6th Tone, which is the most incredibly mutative of all the 
Tones, the ones that I hope I can live long enough to see even a taste of it, this is 
the sacrifice of the individual life.  It is the deepest acceptance of what it is to be 
alive in a way that we cannot and will never truly understand.   
 
I do have a sense that within human beings there is this possibility in the 6 to have a 
much deeper psychic connection to others and a psychic connection rooted in, ob-

Mountains 
Okay, let's move forward.  Let's move up now to the landscape and mountains.  Do 
we have any mountains?  Is there nobody here to take the high ground? 
 
Comment: My husband is a mountain, but that’s as close as I get. 
 
Ra: All right, I‘ll speak for mountains.  I‘m not a mountain person.  It‘s obvious to 
me when we‘re dealing with landscape one of the things that we need to grasp is 
that this is highly generalized, much more easy, in that sense, to deconstruct.  One 
of the keys of mountains, though, is oxygen.  It really is.  One of the most important 
things about these beings is that their place is not only a physical orientation, in that 
sense, but it is one that is truly connected to an environmental factor, that is, it's 
connected to the level of oxygen that's available.   
 
It's not healthy for these people, as an example, to live at sea level.  It just isn‘t.  It 
is very important for them to be able to get to the high ground because they need 
the thinning out of the oxygen in order for them to be able to function correctly.  
When you think about how significant oxygen is, remember what we got to see when 
we were looking at the Internal.  When we were looking at the Internal we got to see 
this progression of sensitivity as you move from the 1 to the 6.  And again, you have 
the same thing.  This is an evolutionary pattern, after all.  We‘re moving out of the 
cave and eventually to the shores.  It sort of sounds like Star Trek, or 2001 Space 
Odyssey when the caveman throws his bone in the sky and it becomes one of these 
space ships. 
 
This is an evolutionary process.  There is a sensitivity here that begins with moun-
tains and it's a sensitivity to the actual environment itself.  In other words, this isn't 
about whether you're inside or outside, it is actually about oxygen.  And it's about 
thinning out oxygen.  One of the things that‘s interesting about mountain people is 
that you‘re going to discover that mountain people are probably some of the heaviest 
smokers of people who smoke because one of the byproducts of nicotine and tobacco 
anyway is that it reduces the level of oxygen in the neo-cortex.  And don‘t say that 
Ra recommended this because somebody will lynch me for it, but if you're a 4 and 
you have to live at sea level and you have no other choice, I suggest you smoke a 
pack a day, because at least you‘ll reduce the oxygen in your brain.  But that‘s really 
what the whole mountain thing is about. 
 
Of course, you can see that the active/passive in this context gives you a much dee-
per understanding of active/passive.  In other words, think about those themes 
where there is a cave-in in the cave and everybody is worried about the fact that 
there's no oxygen to breathe and everybody's telling everybody else to calm down, 
because if you calm down you‘re going to use less oxygen, which means you‘re going 
to live a little longer.  And of course, when you‘re active you're using up a lot more 
oxygen, in that sense.  But it‘s all oxygen related.

Yes, the mountain did come to Muhammad, indeed.  Anyway, so the mountain for 
me is something that is very unique.  It opens up an environmental sensitivity that 
goes beyond in these Internal constructs that are there in the caves, the markets, 
and the kitchens.  Here we‘re stepping out into the larger environment, which means 
that the criteria are different.   
 
The most interesting thing about oxygen depletion is that oxygen depletion leads to 
altered states of consciousness.  Aldous Huxley in his experiments, all of this was 
about reducing oxygen in the neo-cortex.  Anything that reduces oxygen in the neo-
cortex will create an event.  It‘s one of the things that happens when people take 
LSD, as an example.  You have a depletion of oxygen in the neo-cortex; it leads to, 
amongst other things, hallucinations, altered states of perception.   
 
So, when you're dealing with oxygen people you're really looking at this mountain in 
this context.  The worst kind of thing is for these people to have oxygen therapies.  
The higher they are the better they are.  And obviously because they are environ-
mentally sensitive, they don't do well in polluted cities, even though they can get to 
the high ground.  It's one of those things about mountains is that yes, you can de-
construct it; put the child on the upper floor when they‘re sick, and so forth and so 
on.  But the reality is that it is very, very deeply connected to oxygen intake.  So, it‘s 
something to keep in mind about it. 
 
All right, do we have any valley people or any comments? 
 
Question: I want to first ask a question about this oxygen depletion.  I think when 
you just now said that it allows people to go to higher states of consciousness, I just 
keep trying to think of what each one of these things brings to the evolutionary 
process, and so I'm guessing that the main thing mountains do is allow us to think 
differently so we could expand the maya.  Am I on the right track? 
 
Ra: Oh, expand the maya and more than that, Cathy, you hit the nail on the head, 
because of course when you think about mountains that's where the gods were, al-
ways.  The gods were always on the mountains.  We were always climbing towards 
the top of the mountain.  We were always trying to reach out to the gods. 
 
And of course, remember that when we‘re dealing with the nodal External we‘re al-
ways dealing with social complexities of life.  And so this mountain and this increase 
in the potential of consciousness is the expanding of consciousness into the larger 
world.  And it's trying to find the Godhead.  It‘s looking towards the stars.  It's all of 
that.  And of course, you can see what happens that you start that journey of climb-
ing upwards and of course, you end up having to climb back down, anyway.  There's 
a world to be lived in.   
 
But I think in many ways these beings, if they're correct, would have a tendency to 
mysticism and to magic and to drugs and to many other things.  Again, though, 
within the social context and obviously when it's correct.  So, they‘re all fascinating 
to me.  It really lays out the whole infrastructure of the way in which we‘re supposed 
emerge as a consciousness field.

Valleys 
All right, valley people can we have some valley? 
 
Ra: Yeah, they‘re low on the rung, Kumud; tough with them.  Okay, any other val-
ley? 
 
Comment: I’m valley, but I’m not sure that my mike is adequate.  Are you able to 
hear me? 
 
Ra:  Yes, yes, we‘re able to hear you. 
 
Comment: I think that was a valley joke.  I am Right-fixed valley, 5th Color, 6th Tone 
and for me I have a lot of questions about this subject.  I spent 23 years of my life in 
a very high elevation, vast valley, a giant valley.  This valley was so huge that in 
parts of it you could look over the curvature of the Earth as mountains were receding 
120 miles in the distance. 
 
This environment was very empowering for me.  It’s like there was an acoustic den-
sity there, but it was an acoustic density of silence that the words disappeared and 
the spaces between the words became an acoustic density.  It was a very empower-
ing place. 
 
Since I moved to Santa Fe, Santa Fe is a city that is built kind of on the side of a 
slope coming off of the mountain.  I have been very challenged in my health since 
I’ve been here.  I’m just wondering if you could make some comments and observa-
tions. 
 
Santa Fe is on the side of a hill.  There are some valley elements to it, but it’s been 
very challenging to my health.  Also, being in the outer acoustic density is really hard 
for me.  There needs to be silence in this vast valley.  Some comments please. 
 
Ra: I think one of the most important things to grasp about valley is that it‘s not 
about like mountain, it‘s not about oxygen.  The valley is about pressure.  The valley 
that you talk about, when you stand in a valley like that there is this enormous pres-
sure.  Any valley floor, it doesn‘t matter whether the valley floor is suspended at 
14,000 feet or the valley floor is at sea level, it doesn‘t make any difference because 
it isn‘t about being down at the bottom, it‘s about the pressure that is there. 
 
Of course, these are beings that need to be attuned to exactly the right pressure.  
Do you remember when they introduced Samadhi Tanks?  Samadhi Tank was a place 
where you floated in water that was exactly set to your body temperature so in a 
sense you literally let go and disappeared.  You ended up floating in space.  I think 
fundamentally that‘s what valleys are all about.  That is, this place of finding the per-
fect pressure field, the specific density of pressure is something that is very, very 
important to these beings.  And it‘s only within this kind of pressure field that the 
benefits of their system and the benefits of their interaction really get revealed.

Question: So, following this thread about how it evolves us, how does the pressure 
evolve us? 
 
Ra: Population.  Look at any given city in which you have 8 or 9,000 people living in 
a building complex.  This is an enormous pressure field.  When you‘re on the bottom 
of that, there are some human beings that will absolutely thrive in being in that kind 
of pressure.  It is part of our vast expansion.  It isn‘t just simply the physical baro-
metric pressure, which is the physical essence of it.  It is all the other pressures.   
 
These are beings that have to find the right pressure environment.  Is it right to be 
in the madness of the core of a city?  Is that the pressure that is correct for these 
beings?  Is it the pressure that is there in the emptiness of an environment in which 
the whole environment, the night—I‘ve lived up in that country—the night itself is 
this huge pressure of starlight beating down on you as if it were ready to fall on top 
of you.  It‘s about attuning the vehicle to the right pressure.  But that pressure phys-
ically is always something that needs to be in the hollow of something, within the 
embrace of something, like being in the cauldron, in that sense. 
 
Shores 
So, we can close without forgetting anything.  I‘m shores, so I guess I can talk for 
shores.  And I‘m natural shores.  I‘ve always been so deeply aware of an odd ano-
maly about me that I was aware of long before I had this knowledge.  I liked being 
close to water, but didn‘t like going in.  I grew up in Canada and we all learned how 
to swim and we all have to go through all these various levels and proficiency and 
blah, blah, blah.  I went through all of that process.  But what I really noticed in my 
life is that I loved being near water, but I just wasn‘t really interested in the water. 
 
I was born on an island.  Montreal is an island in the St. Lawrence River.  I‘ve lived 
on many islands.  For nearly the last 25 years now I have had Ibiza as my base.  It‘s 
clear to me that it is something that has been a great serendipity in my life.  There is 
something deeply healing for me.  The moment that I step on to the continent I feel 
physically challenged.  It‘s very different the way my body reacts.   
 
Being a shore person is life on the edge.  There is an edge here; there is always an 
edge.  There‘s an abyss on the other side.  And the shores are varied.  You can live 
in the suburbs on the shores of the city.  You can live in exurbia on the shores of the 
suburbs.  You can live in the country.  You can live on the banks of the river or the 
side of a lake.  There are many, many shores.  
 
But it says something very fundamental about the greatest expansion of humans and 
the densest environments in which we find humans is by shores.  Every great city in 
the world, every great older city in the world is built on a river or built on a shore.  
They all are.  It becomes places in which the huge confluences of life meet.   
 
After all, these are about environments.  From the moment that you move from the 
mountains and away from the Godhead and you move down into the expanding

The Difference between Natural and Artificial Shores 
The difference between natural and artificial, as the Zen master would say, is the dif-
ference between natural and artificial.  There are artificial boundaries, there are ar-
tificial shores.  There are walls that separate a property from another.  You can live 
on that shore; you can have a shoreline.  You can have an artificial fence.  You can 
have a highway that separates this side of the environment from that side of the en-
vironment.  What used to be the different side of the tracks, as it were. 
 
Natural is natural.  There are places where things naturally come to an end, where 
cities end.  Is a house artificial?  I would guess so.  I certainly would guess so.  I 
can‘t see how it‘s going to be natural.  Yes, sure, Elfriede, help yourself.  Oh, you 
want me to say something about wide valleys. 
 
The Difference between Narrow and Wide Valleys 
Well, again, I don‘t mean to be facetious, but there‘s a huge difference between nar-
row and wide.  For example, in my American language a gorge would be quite nar-
row.  The kind of valley that you can see the curvature of the Earth, as Genoa de-
scribes, would be quite wide.  Again, these are very pure keynotes.  So the dilemma

Exam Preparation

perience going through it the first time to learn the lessons.  I want you to have a 
sense of being prepared.

My focus in this second exam is the basic focus of the Internal and the External.  
This is the focus.  That is, the focus on the PHS regimen, the focus on the cognition, 
the focus on the environment.  In other words, these are the areas that I'm con-
cerned about.  Now obviously, as I mentioned from the very beginning this is a pro-

In most cases the problem was the parent, but there was nothing I could do with the 
parents.  Their assumption was that they were normal and they were okay, and it 
was their child that was having a problem.  And for them, the problem is homoge-
nized; they didn‘t see it in any other way.  It was very difficult talking to the parents. 
I stopped doing that after awhile because it was pointless and it was depressing. 
 
This is the thing that you realize when you become a parent.  I think it is one of the 
things that is so touching, in that moment of becoming a parent you realize that this 
tiny thing is so utterly dependent on you, and that its life depends on you, and to 
take that in very deeply.  It‘s so obvious to me that it's not that these parents don't 
want what's good for their child, but they want what‘s good for their child within the 
homogenized context.  Without awareness, without correctness it doesn't work.  It 
just doesn't work.  That‘s always the lesson; it‘s the most important lesson in the 
end.   
 
It‘s why I'm so cautious about various levels of this knowledge in terms of the way in 
which it's presented outward.  That is, you all know this, it takes a great deal of 
physical and intellectual dedication to be a part of this process.  It really does.  And 
not everybody's ready for that.  It is a limited fractal; that is the way it is.  And to 
really be able to benefit children, work on their parents.  There‘s nothing else to do, 
because that will make a difference.  That will change everything.  It will change 
their lives and that‘s part of the work. 
 
Written question: If you have a quadruple Left being is it appropriate to take them 
to Color transfer for Personality motivation first when the PHS is just not attracting 
them? 
 
Ra: Yes, I would, Kumud.  All Left struggle deeply with the homogenized conscious-
ness.  The advantage of the strategic is that if you teach them a trick, boy do they 
know how to use it.  And to be so strategic that it would be something—again, given 
that they're following their Strategy and Authority—it would make much more sense 
to point them towards Rave Psychology in the same way that somebody who is 
quadruple Right that you have to rush them to the door of PHS. 
 
To all of you, I wish you well.  The timing, by the way, will be the same as the last 
time.  It will be 15 minutes after the hour, so, you are more than welcome to come 
into the class, and the class will stay open during the test.  We have upgraded all of 
it in terms of mailing it to you in terms of the way that I can mark it and you can see 
my markings and the final results and so forth and so on.  I'll do my best to write a 
fair and interesting exam and let's see what happens.  But remember most of all, it‘s 
the process.  The joy is in the journey, so, enjoy the journey.  Until next time; bye 
for now.       
 
~
